Tested before changing which was a filter needing changed & then ran 5 or more gallons thru this one before I saw any difference.. and still red, not safe to drink. So sad we pay for poison water from our towns... maybe we go to water companies & demand clean same water that's at least safe to bathe in. Oh well , back to the drawing board. For now I bought...
persona: This customer prioritizes product reliability and proper fit above all else, willing to pay a fair price for a well-constructed replacement that matches the performance of a trusted brand, and values clear packaging and shipping protection to prevent damage.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Might be better to go with the original. I bought a package of 3 and this is the first time I have used this brand. It was to replace Golden Pure brand.It appears that the plastic lugs may be of a too soft material and it either damaged the filter housing or the filter was not of good quality. I've had no problem of replacing the filter before so I know it' not something I did. I also tried a second one and had the same results. Leaked water all over our floor. I trashed them and now it looks like I will be out $80.00 plus labor for a new housing.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great buy Finally found a great filter system for my indoor dryer unit, our home finally is pleasant and clear of lints, I will definitely recommend this product to a friend. Review 2: rating 5.0. Didn't work for me. This made my dryer overheat and constantly turn off. I finally gave up on it and took it off. Not worth the money. Review 3: rating 4.0. Only choice but worked Couldn't use a regular dryer vent so gave this a try. It heats up the room which is nice for winter but not for summer. Review 4: rating 5.0. It’s a good indoor vent for your dryer Easy to install It was overpriced when I bought this. I paid $80 for indoor Dryer vent Wish it was a little bit cheaper Review 5: rating 3.0. Hose will blow off I like the "no more" lint flying around. Double screen collects lint. The only issue is the connection from the back of the dryer to the end of the hose. It blows off after 10 minutes of the dryer running. The instructions state push the dryer after connecting the house back to the wall. Unfortunately I have pipes on the wall that prevent that from happening. You can't put a hose clamp on due to the piece being hard plastic. There is a band of silicone that goes ov... Review 6: rating 5.0. Pleasantly Suprised I took a chance on this indoor dryer vent, as it was time to replace the one I had, as it was pretty old model.
I installed this myself as soon as I got it, and followed very good instructions that came with the vent, which was very appreciated, as they were very simple and clear. It is working out very well in the room (my washer and dryer is in our basement laundry room). Since it is easier to reach now, I can clean it every time I use it, and keep it moni... Review 7: rating 5.0. Very Happy I am almost 60, I live in a mobile home, and heat with wood and propane. Winters are cold and I hate wasting energy, and didn't want to crawl under home and connect a long dryer hose to a vent and cut a hole in metal under penning. My son put up for me behind dryer in 15 mins. And attached new hose I bought. It puts heat and humidity back into home, and filter takes care of dust issue. I am very happy with this unit! So glad I found it, now if I have to use the dryer... Review 8: rating 5.0. The filter in the water reservoir There’s nothing I dislike about this I like the filtration unit on it because the only place I can put my dryer vent is on that little wall and I do not want it to be non-filtered so that my brand new AC unit will fill up with lint because the AC unit Flows from the wall to the outdoor I think this is going to be a win-win product and for the price you can’t beat it Review 9: rating 5.0. Works well! I had a cheap floor inside venting container and it wasn't working well for me. I didn't have high hopes for this one but I am pleasantly surprised. It has cut down on the humidity that results from indoor venting the dryer. It is still humid, but much better than the floor models available. This was easy to install and easy to clean and fill. Review 10: rating 3.0. The Relief valve is set way to loose You will need to adjust the tabs on the relief "valve" to make sure it doesn't pop open the moment there is even the least bit of pressure. Review 11: rating 5.0. Great way to save heat. This product is great for saving the heat from your dryer. The filter really works keeping lint and dust out of the room. The water tray seems to do double duty by adding moisture in the room and catching small lint that doesn't get in the filter. It was very easy to install. Over all I am very happy. Review 12: rating 5.0. Love this in winter keeps heat in home I use this in the winter only it adds humidity and heat to home. The filters work well and keep mess with dust down. Easy to install and clean. Would recommend that you have two dryer hoses and a little insulation that you can use to plug off vent to outside when disconnected. Review 13: rating 5.0. Perfect! We bought a house that was built in 1974 recently, so we've ran into lots of things that aren't up to today's codes. A major one was the installed dryer vent. The laundry room is in the central hall, far from outside walls. Previous owners had the vent running into walls, out of walls, then a 30 foot long run to an outside wall. I'm surprised they never burned the house down with that. So we installed this item on the wall above our dryer. Just add a bit of water and clean t... Review 14: rating 4.0. REMEMBER HEAT EXHAUST nice little get up... easy install, common sense goes along way here... So let me tell you where my common sense wasn't ... I have it mounted on my basement interior wall, and well, in my not so big basement, it gets a little warm in there lol... keep our house at 72 and the basement, obviously colder most times, will be 15 degrees hotter than upstairs when my wife is runnin her dryer.... Outside of this bein an easy fix er generally easy and avoidable my wife... Review 15: rating 2.0. Worked untill it was full. This product worked great, until the filter got full of lint. Per instructions there was only 1 weight needed when installing, so thats what was on there. Instead of the flap opening to notify me it needed to be replaced, it popped the whole filter off breaking a piece of plastic on the bottom so now there is a space between the water tray and the filter when put back together....
Disappointing because now im not sure what i am going to use as a dryer ... Review 16: rating 5.0. Works Wanted this unit to help humidify and keep heat in the house. Works well and I like the water tray to which I add aromatherapy oils. The unit is pretty expensive for a plastic box, but it's effective. Comes with a few replacement pads. Review 17: rating 5.0. Worth the money Works super well, easy to install, done 6 loads so far and filter still isnt clogged Review 18: rating 5.0. Easy to install. I like the removable metal screen because it will save on replacing filters. I am also using it as an auxiliary heat source for my basement. Review 19: rating 5.0. Exactly what I needed! I got really tired of the old bucket type lint catcher! Every time I had to empty it, it was a messy job and the lint bucket never would stay attached to the wall. This one is easy to install and works perfect! I highly recommend! Review 20: rating 5.0. Keeps the lint out of the house Great to keep the lint out of the room/house while allowing the heat and moisture (remember it's dry in the winter) to diffuse into the room. Find a drier hose with solid ends (not just clamps) so it's easy to move the hose from this box to the outside vent when it gets hot. Only difficulty was predrilling the holes to screw this to the wall (the box is deep for an electric drill).
persona: This customer prioritizes functional performance and value for money, favoring products that deliver effective results despite minor flaws; they are willing to make small adjustments or compromises (like using tape or extended cycles) to achieve practical benefits, and they appreciate compact, space-efficient designs that fit into tight living areas.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Keeps heat in house Great product!!! My vent hose was smaller then opening but I put some duck tape around it to seal it and it works amazing. It was so cold in the room then I started doing the drying of cloths and the whole room heated up nicely, heat even made it to the living room. Omg winter just started and this product is going to save so much on heating cost.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. Don’t waste your money. I want to love this as an option. I was excited to have coffee that didn’t obtain micro plastics or cockroaches. I use a finely ground coffee and there are no coffee grinds in the bottom of my cups, which I had with previous plastic cups. It is a pleasure to be able to drink the final swig in my cup! The cup works nicely in my Keurig Mini Coffee maker. Review 8: rating 2.0. I love the product because it’s super sturdy and the mesh is fine to hold the contents. But I don’t like that. The lid comes off when you’re trying to get it out of the machine. Sorry, but it would've definitely been a 5- star because of the quality of the product. I love the stainless steel because that will last forever. I just don't like that my cont... They clean up very easily and my coffee tastes great with them too. No worrying about ingesting plastic when using these k-cups Review 10: rating 2.0. Kinda wish I had bought one which is operated with a wall switch. Review 2: rating 5.0. Fantech Dryer Booster EXCELLENT!

Keep in mind that each 90° angle is equal to approximately 5-7 feet of straight run pipe. After discussing with a contractor, I found out that I needed a dryer booster fan based upon the venting distance.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Must have for long vents This did the trick.  My dryer is on an inside wall.  The vent goes about 15' up the wall into the attic then about 25' across the top of the garage to vent out the side of the house.  It was taking 2 1/2 hrs per load for normal clothes and 3 to 5 hrs for jeans, towells, etc to dry.  Now it takes 30 to 45 minutes max!!  Highly recommend if you have a long vent.  I installed this in the attic and it runs very quiet.  I can hear it for a few minutes pulling air through the dryer a few minutes after the dryer shuts off.  I did have to make the sensor a little less sensetive.  It was turning on I guess from wind blowing through the vent from the outside.  a small adjustment and all is working great.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Kitchen Bath collection Island Range Hood The Island Range Hood looks very modern from the 35 year old hood we took out. Our kitchen is open to Living room
and this hood blocks less of the view from the kitchen, then the old avocado green hood did. It run much smoother than the old one. A little less noise than the old one also. It came well packed for shipping and arrived earlier than expected. Had a little corner damage on the stainless steel enclosure part. Not enough to be a problem. The corner damage may have happen a the factory, because it was packed well. The LED light are good. I do wish they could be manually directed to more of the center part of the cook top. All and All the hood is a good product for the price.

persona: This customer prioritizes functional performance and durability for a specific task, willing to pay a reasonable price for a tool that excels in its intended use, but is frustrated by poor long-term build quality even when the product meets core needs.
reference_output:
rating 4.0. Great tool at a low price I needed something for a primary purpose: pushing things through the disposal's splash guard. I don't typically look for the cheapest thing out there, but look for the best thing for the job. Others cost more, but would hardly bankrupt anybody. Yet this one does the job and more. It sits in the opening in a way that won't damage the splash guard below. It can be left so that the bottom ring is flush with the flange, so water drains and most particles will wash down the drain, yet items won't fall in. It's practical for pushing larger items, can be pushed down to use as a stopper, and comes with multiple rings so appropriately sized ones can be used.

Setting it up merely required putting on the rings. The instructions hinted that their tips would make it easier, giving me the impression that it would be harder than I might have hoped for without instructions. Yet it was easy enough that no instructions or tricks were really needed. The rings did all come pressed together and at first glance they seemed to have multiple grooves, but once I pulled them apart from each other, the rest was simple.

I had initially considered buying this together with a few alternatives so I could see which one I liked best. Then I decided that if I didn't like this one, it was cheap enough that I could always buy something else if it didn't work out. I'm glad that I got this first. It's everything I need.

persona: This customer prioritizes space-saving, multi-functional appliances that solve specific home comfort issues like humidity control and laundry convenience, and values automated features that enhance safety and ease of use.
reference_output:
rating 5.0. I love this little machine! Space is at a premium in my condo unit and I'd lose too much valuable storage with a full size front loader and dryer. But I also hate hauling loads of laundry up and down the elevator. I also don't like the fact that the machines in my building's laundry room only rinse once. The Comfee allows multiple rinses.

So I was glad to realize that by rearranging some shelves in my bathroom linen closet, I'd have room to store this smaller Comfee 0.9 cubic foot washer. I attach the washer supply hose to my bathroom vanity faucet and drain into my bathtub.

I do most of my laundry this way now. It spins most of the water out so clothes are damp but not dripping. I drape them over a collapsible bamboo drying rack or hang items up on plastic hangers to air dry. Items come out wrinkled so I usually snap and hand smooth the wrinkles out. I touch up dress shirts with an iron.

Now I only need to use building’s laundry room for big bulky items like quilts or winter coats.

My bathroom is cramped, so along with the washer, I ordered a 4 wheel appliance dolly to make it easier to move the unit in and out of the closet. I also ordered a 10 foot burst-proof hose and a couple of 90 degree elbow fittings (one at each end of the supply hose). Having extra hose length and elbow fittings made it easier to step over the hose when moving about.

ONE ISSUE: I did have a problem with the quick connect adapter provided. It fit my vanity faucet OK, but I often had trouble getting the supply hose to latch onto it properly. So I abandoned the quick connect and ordered a simple adapter to allow me to just screw on the hose fitting when I'm going to do washing.

CAUTION: The output volume of water when the machine drains may overflow smaller sinks like my bathroom vanity. I closely watched my very first load and determined my vanity sink would not drain fast enough. I had to pause the wash machine’s drain cycle half way to allow my sink to catch-up. So I fashioned a temporary "stand pipe" for my bathtub by using a piece of 2" rigid black plastic pipe and some Velcro straps to attach it to a vertical grab bar. The top of the stand pipe is within the height range specified in the manual and the bottom is a couple inches below the rim of the bathtub.

UPDATE 12/29/2022 - Managing lint: Instructions are ambiguous on how often to clean the lint filter (the one inside the drum). Manual suggests once a month in one place but every 10 cycles elsewhere. I've found it wise to check more often. I have many cotton clothing items which seem to shed a lot of fibers in the wash. I also found it necessary to put a lint filter on my bathtub drain to catch the fibers that make it out through the drain hose.

But I'm still quite happy with overall performance of this washer.

ACCESORIES:

4 wheel dolly.

https://www.amazon.com/dp/B08PNQW3RP?ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_product_details&th=1

Simple hose adapter (replaced the troublesome quick-connect adapter) .

https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09SG6LLRD?psc=1&ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_product_details

10 foot burst proof supply hose.

https://www.amazon.com/dp/B077XT5ZY3?psc=1&ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_product_details

Two 90 degree supply elbows. These elbows come with a bright yellow soft plastic grip on the female side. That makes it easy to grip and tighten the hose to my faucet. But it gets in the way of attaching the other elbow to the washing machine. Fortunately you can pry the soft grip off so the bare metal coupling fits the machine. Since I leave the supply hose attached to the machine all the time, this is not an issue for me. When done washing, I disconnect from the faucet and wind the free end of the long hose inside the machine tub. I use a piece of pool noodle to prop the lid partway open so it doesn't pinch the hoses and it allows the tub to dry out.

https://www.amazon.com/dp/B08M6DY7Q8?ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_product_details&th=1

Update: still doesn't work with routines, but it can trigger automation using ifttt. Bumping my... Review 20: rating 5.0. Essential in every home The Yolink temperature sensor is essential in promptly detecting temperature excursions in any location. We use one on the refrigerator, and one in the freezer. While these sensors do not integrate with Alexa, the Yolnik app can be programmed to send email and text messages when temperature limits are exceeded.
persona: This customer values functional reliability and practical performance in products, particularly those that meet specific usage needs like precise temperature monitoring or consistent ice-making in unique environments such as an RV, and they are willing to accept minor drawbacks like size or setup complexity for dependable, task-focused results.
reference_output: rating 5.0. These do work but -- Nothing I can say overly negative about these temp sensors. They do what they are supposed to do. I put one in my fridge and one in the freezer. Setting these up is not a one step process. When the fridge goes through it's defrost cycles there is a temp drop inside so you have to allow for this. I ended up setting the alarms at higher temp settings to allow for this. Be aware that moving the sensor in different spots can change the reading so once you set it and you are happy with it, don't move the sensor to another shelf. You may have to start all over again with making alarm adjustments. To set this up and make adjustments can take a bit of time. The only complaint I have is that there is a hanger slot in the back but it really doesn't make for easily hanging in a fridge. I have seen other temp sensors that had suction cups on the back. That would be a great improvement to these sensors.

But then it leaves you with the top of the pod still attached to the filter with the coffee grounds inside, the plastic ring and the foil top. It is very difficult to detach the filter (which is quite strong!) from the plastic ring. This makes it difficult to remove the coffee grounds for composting. Solution...make the razor cutters slightly longer so they would cut through the fil... Review 7: rating 5.0. Helps to recycle Works great. Can help recycle k-cups instead of going to landfill. I'm all about recycling and saving the environment, but I think you could probably just use a sharp knife instead. It cuts the plastic around the coffee/filter inside the K-cup easily so that the filter is not cut exposing the grounds, so that's helpful. Review 12: rating 5.0. reference_output: rating 5.0. Good product if your recycler takes K cups This works well to separate the inner coffee pouch from the outer plastic cup. It takes a little practice to cut at the right depth and not cut too deep that you spill out the grounds. However, check your recycling company before buying. A few weeks after I purchased this, our recycler said they would no longer accept small plastic items like the K cups.

reference_output:
rating 1.0. Terrible filter afer two months! Buy the Whirlpool filter and save in the long run!!!! We installed this filter on our Whirlpool Refrigerator and two months later the water was only trickling. I reseated it just in case something had gotten in the line. Nothing had gotten in. The filter was plugged after two months. The valve and all are fine. After reseating water was still trickling. We ordered and installed the factory Whirlpool filter and as expected the water flow is normal. The waster valve made noise in the days that this cheap filter was clogged, think of the damage that does over time! Why take a chance with this cheaper unit coming apart inside and risking a Service Call because it damaged the valve that is hard to get to?????

PAY TYHE MONEY. YOU BOUGHT A NICE FRIDGE. BUY THE WHIRLPOOL FACTORY FILTER. IT IS YOUR HEALTH, IF THIS FILTER CLOGGS AFTER TWO MONTHS THEN IT IS FAILING INSIDE. The old whirlpool filter went over one year not changed and never had an issue. I now have the Whirlpool filter in my subscribe-n-save every six months.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Just what I needed Worked like a charm and was just what we needed. Price was reasonable and quality seemed excellent, but I'm no expert. Received it right away! Thanks, Review 2: rating 5.0. Works Great The belt is either original or equal to Samsung belt. Installed weeks ago and no problem, highly recommended and saves money. Review 3: rating 3.0. Samsung Dryer Belt This purchase is the third time in three years that I have replaced the dryer belt on my Samsung dryer. The belts are not expensive and are relatively easy to replace. The design is rather poor. The belt tends to break every 4 months or so due to the high temperature of the drum. Review 4: rating 5.0. Perfect Match for my Samsung Dryer We bought our electric Samsung Dryer (DV218AEW/XAA) new in December 2009. We've had no problems with either the dryer or the washer... It's been approximately 3 years 8 months to today until the dryer stopped working.

The dryer's electronics would turn on, but the motor/drum would not spin. No sounds... No error codes... Nothing...

After looking around a bit online, people said it was common for the dryer belt to fail. After re... Review 5: rating 5.0. Confusing to install, but awesome to have my dryer back! This belt will appear to be waaaaaay too long when you install it -- the trick is realizing that it zig-zags 'the long way' around the pullies. It took me quite a while to figure that out. Once you get it installed, though, it works like a charm! Review 6: rating 2.0. Barely a year later, and it's making weird noises again... I may try a linked belt system this time, because the installation is needlessly complicated. Poor design, poor reliability, very disappointing. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great Fast Service My dryer belt died on a Friday. I ordered the part the same day, and received it on Monday.

I would have had the dryer fixed Monday night but for the fact that I'm clueless sometimes and didn't realize that a part had come undone inside the dryer. I watched the Youtube video and thought I had threaded the belt properly, but each time I tried it the drum wouldn't turn. I took the dryer apart, put the belt on, and put it together again about 6 times and... Review 8: rating 3.0. Not a "Samsung" brand belt, but compatible replacement. Would have given it 5 stars except for this fact.

Went on the dryer no problem, though, and is working perfectly. Price is competitive and good. Shipped quickly and arrived promptly from the seller. Again, really no problem except that it's a... Watch out for sharp edges of sheetmetal when installing though. Save a ton of money and do it yourself. Thank you. This belt works on both the front loading Samsung washer and dryer.

Review 1: rating 1.0. Cheap junk I ended up taking it apart and using a bearing out of it to fix my oem pump Review 2: rating 5.0. Works great in a KDTE104DSS0 This motor was a perfect OEM replacement for my KitchenAid dishwasher. You can actually leave the whole bottom subassembly intact and remove the defective motor by turning it a quarter turn. You can then remove the new motor from the impeller housing using a quarter turn and just replace it in the assembly. That way you do not have to worry about recrimping the clamps. Review 3: rating 5.0. This does replace/fit for W11032770. There is a long, long story that goes with this review, but I'm going to spare you and just say the important parts.

This replaced and fit perfectly on my Whirlpool WDF770SAFZ0 machine. The replacement part called for part number W11032770, but this looks and fits exactly the same. I can't really figure out what's different (if anything).

Replacing it was simple. I just flipped over the machine and removed a few screwed on it... Review 4: rating 1.0. After removing the dishwasher floor panel, we discovered that the pump was leaking water onto the floor whenever the dishwasher was running. If you use the hose clamp the pump comes with it requires a special crimping tool which cost $62 at Home Depot. Good luck ... Review 9: rating 5.0. It works! I could hear the unit go through all cycles, but the circulation pump cycle did not circulate water. After a YouTube video viewing, it was apparent this was an easy replacement. You can then remove the new motor from the impeller housing using a quarter turn and just replace it in the assembly. That way you do not have to worry about recrimping the clamps. reference_output:
rating 4.0. Part numbering schemes... I have a ~2014 model year Frigidaire dishwasher and the motor started leaking from the housing. I pulled it out and noted the part number was 154853801 which costs anywhere between 125 - 200$ to buy a replacement. I started seeing mixed messages on Amazon and other parts sites online which made me take chance and buy this part 154844101. Sure enough this motor is identical to the one I pulled out from the dishwasher, and the little gold label on the item I received actually read part# 154853801. Turns out to squeeze more money out of customers, the manufacturer swapped part numbers starting from a certain model year forward. To add to the insult Frigidaire wants ~60$ to replace the 2 rubber o-rings if you buy the model 154853801, that are sold separately. With this part 154844101, the o-rings are included. Don't subject yourself to this sort of usury if you have a newer model dishwasher that uses the 154853801 part. Buy this part instead.
And if your o-rings are all that have gone bad, go to an auto-parts or hardware store and buy exact replacement o-rings for under 4$. This ridiculous pricing scheme, the latest motor failure and a prior failure with the water inlet valve which caused a leak, mean I wont be buying Frigidaire/Electrolux anything in the future.

persona: This customer values affordable, high-quality reusable coffee pods that offer convenience and reliability, prioritizing practicality and long-term usability over price alone, and is likely to repurchase based on consistent performance and functional benefits.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Quality, inexpensive replacement or extra reusable coffee pods These are nice extra pods for your Keurig. I have another set of black ones, but only had four. We used to be sticklers and only grind coffee right before brewing it, but I now do so for the sake of convenience (also the reason we bought a Keurig instead of another Bunn when it died). I wanted more pods so I could have some ready to go while others were still drying after I emptied and rinsed them. Like most such pods,  these tend to tip over if you don't lean the lid on something until it's filled isn't so top-heavy. My solution is to prop them up against the cup platform on the Keurig machine. I use a small funnel and that makes quick work of filling the pods. I keep the prepared pods in a Rubbermaid storage container to keep everything fresh. Any extra coffee left when I grind the beans goes in a smaller container and with the pods. Note that the coffee will not brew correctly with these pods if you lose the inner ring on the lid. Water will go over the edge and not into the pod.

rating 5.0. Absolutely the best bang for the buck! Fast same day delivery with Prime and the filter fit like a glove in my Samsung fridge. All good so far.

The one thing that was different and I wasn’t prepared for, was the air in the line having so much pressure that water went everywhere during the “bleeding the line” procedure. Running a gallon out was perfect as the by the 3/4 point the water was crystal clear and all the air was out of the line.

I understand that some people had black water when they were initially running the gallon out but mine was only slightly discolored and no black anywhere.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Great for small apartments, but it's smallish I had a combo washer/spin dryer unit for my small apartment that died on me a few weeks ago & decided to get this to replace it. I did not realize it was somewhat smaller in load size than my prior unit.
No major issues so far with this unit, but surprised me it's notably smaller than my prior unit, but no major issue with me.

PROS: >>Washes clothes quickly & cleans them well
>>Spin dryer works very well
>>Operating knobs are contoured & the best easiest to grasp control knobs I have seen on these combo units
>>Very quiet operation of washer
CONS: >>AC cord too short [3 feet long only] & had to use water/weatherproof 6 foot extension cord
>>Inlet water hose too small for bathtub spigot & had to devise a turkey baster to do the job to fill wash tub
with water using a bucket
>>Spin dryer can get out of balance easily when spin drying clothes, balance load before spin drying please
>>No lint filter trap in wash tub

This unit works well & does what it's suppose to do. It does get my clothes pretty clean & dries rather well in the unheated spin dryer.
5 stars I rate this item as it works good enough to get the job done quickly.

persona: This customer values a well-made, visually appealing product but prioritizes realistic performance over exaggerated claims, and is likely to be cautious about marketing promises when making purchase decisions.
reference_output: rating 3.0. Cool looking machine, but 20 plus pounds of ice a day is not realistic... Okay, so heres the deal with this thing... I almost hate to give this 3 stars cause its actually a well made machine and looks really cool. The problem is it just doesn't make enough ice. They through these big numbers out their like 20 plus pounds of ice in 24 hours, but the problem is you would need to LITERALLY need to stand beside this machine and run it for 24 hours. The truth is it makes about a gallon size ziplock bag of ice a day. Again, this is because nobody has time to stand near the machine and empty the tiny tiny basket thats inside of it or continuously fill it with water all day long. So my point is, if you REALLY need 5 to 20 pounds of ice daily, I would look into something more heavy duty.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. You have to play around to get the hang of it, but better than the original pods after that! Was a bit hesitant about buying this, but it has been great! Bought it so I could use it with my Pilon coffee, and to be a bit more environmentally friendly.

The first few tries were very watery. The trick is to add a little more coffee than recommended on the manual. A sweet spot for me as been 1 full measuring spoon and 1/4. This produces beautiful crema and incredible mouthfeel for the espressos, ristrettos, and the dairy drinks. I'm still working on getting a good lungo, but that may just be a problem with my roast.

The capsule itself is very high quality and fit well in my Creatista machine. The pod didn't damage the pod slot, as some people reported. It falls nicely into the pod holder after use.
When removing the lids, make sure you let the pod completely cool off and that you only pull the lid from the lip that sticks out from the seal. Otherwise, the lid's seal will be nearly impossible to remove from the pod.

INSTALLATION

I found the washer in the piping. Headache to tear apart and fix my dishwasher after this part came apart! Buy a different brand! Review 18: rating 5.0. Confirm correct size! These worked great and were very easy to install. But when you buy these replacement wheels, choose the ones with the correct size. I almost bought the ones with the shorter size, but after reading other reviews realized that most of the ones on Amazon were shorter than those on my dishwasher. So measure, measure, measure before you buy! If you replace with the shorter size than your original wheels you may experience interference with the lower spray wand. Review 19: rating 5.0. Perfect replacement I purchased this for my Kenmore elite dishwasher when the stock wheels started falling off because the plastic was deteriorating from the high heat of the dishwasher. reference_output:
rating 2.0. Poor quality control I had to order these dishwasher rack wheels twice. Both times, the plastic frames of the units that the wheels are attached to were so warped/bent, that the wheels were very crooked. Most were so crooked that they wouldn’t roll at all - they just kind of skidded when I tried to move the dish rack.

I had high hopes for replacement wheels that claimed to use stainless steel hubs instead of plastic. Unfortunately, when the frames are warped on two different orders so that the wheels won’t roll, I can’t recommend this product.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Complete repair kit I am happy that this kit contained everything to replace the heating element properly.  It included the high limit fuse, high limit thermostat and the heating element along with the necessary jumper wires and screws.  The new thermostat is held in place with two sheet metal screws (Provided) instead of using the hanger tab and one screw.  There is an included jumper wire to connect the new style thermostat to the power block.  The whole repair took me about 35 minutes because I made sure to clean all the lint out of the ducts and off the back panel.

Review 1: rating 5.0. fast acting! I spent $4200 on a Kitchen aid fridge and have had it a year and a half and have had to have ice maker serviced 4 times now. I like the fact that it cleans itself. If you purchase this and go through a ton of ice like we do, don't forget to buy ice bags on amazon!!! Review 2: rating 5.0. Great ice maker I have used at least 6 ice makers. This machine is by far the best ice maker I have ever had. It makes lots of ice and dumps into a large basket. The controls are easy to read and placed conveniently on the top facing forward. Up to now, my biggest problem with all of these other ice makers is that for the most part they don't last very long. The last 2 other brands lasted 30 and 60 days respectively. This seems to be made much better then any of the others so... Review 3: rating 1.0. DO NOT PURCHASE, READ OTHER REVIEWS, BUYER BEWARE! So far running like a top It works as advertised. I purchased this particular manufacturers device because it had fewer out of the box or nearly out of the box failures. So far expectations have been met.

The only thing that we've noticed is that it holds the ice right at the freezing point of water, perhaps higher and when we've produced ice and placed it in our larger freezer the cubes tend to freeze to one another. This is not a problem for us and the clumps are easil... Review 5: rating 5.0. Great ice, super easy, just slightly wet This ice maker has been a perfect solution for our household that has stopped us from making weekly trips to the corner convenience store for ice. We make a pot of ice tea a day and, combined with other demands, our fridge ice maker could never keep up. Not to mention the fridge ice maker has been repaired four times in seven years. This is the perfect solution. We just make enough to keep the backup bucket in the main freezer full and then ... Review 6: rating 5.0. This thing is great! Bought this to replace another brand that I had for a couple of years and it wasn't performing well. This new one not only makes bigger cubes, it does it in record time. What used to take me a day and a half to get with my old one, I can get in 2 hours with this. It's fantastic. Unless the performance drops or something breaks, it will stay 5 stars! (seems to be common) I have to have ice available as I drink a lot of water and this machine makes more than I could ever need. It's quiet and compact enough to sit by my coffee maker on my coffee bar. If something changes I will update my review but for now it it a 2 thumbs up! Review 9: rating 4.0. Looks to be a nice piece Let me do a cliffs notes then ill elaborate. Basically it holds a little over 3 quarts of water on the first fill, holds about 3 times as much ice as our old magic chef war horse held. It uses an optic eye to stop production which is a huge step up over the thermocouple our old one used. And it has a self clean mode which is essentially just a mode where it runs the water pump to circulate water or vinegar for 6 minutes. Thats about the jist of what separa... Review 10: rating 5.0. We read the the reviews of the various models and finally decided on this Dreamircle. The ice maker is really easy to use. You plug it in, add water and push a button. While it's making... reference_output: rating 1.0. Poor decision based on price I had high hopes for these filters, but was very disappointed that they did not activate the valve that allows water to flow through them in my Samsung refrigerator.  The end piece in the center is what pushes the water valve, but unlike the Samsung filters, which are flat at the end, these have an indentation which does not activate the valve.  Is short, this means that no water flows into the filter.  I tried two of these with the same result.  I have returned the product and ordered original Samsung filters.  The frustration was not worth the savings.

reference_output: rating 5.0. A tight fit for a K150, but workable I bought these to fit in a Keurig 150, which is one of the models listed in the description as compatible. Clean up is a little work as fine ground get caught in the screen mesh, but if you used one of the Kcup-shaped filters, that helps a lot.

persona: This customer prioritizes exact compatibility and OEM-quality replacements, values clear installation instructions, and prefers durable, functional parts that offer a straightforward upgrade over full unit replacements.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Saved our spin dryer The timer knob broke on the mini spin dryer we use for drying bathing suits and we had no luck finding a replacement. This control knob however, was just the right size. The 4 pack comes with 2 different length adapters that accommodate a 1/4 or 5/16" shaft with a flat spot. The shaft on our dryer is round with a pin through it but I was able to drill the adapter to round it out then cut a notch to slide it over the pin. This knob is sturdier than the original one but even if it breaks, I have 3 spares!  It fits snug and now the dryer is useable again.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. So it is best to drop it into a freezer to get it ... So far we are about a week into owning this ice maker. We've used it at home and now it resides on our sailboat and has been tirelessly making ice almost nonstop during the day. You have to understand that these icemakers make ice that is not extremely cold - probably around 26 degrees, so it is best to drop it into a freezer to get it down close to 0. I refer to it as "wet ice" although it's not really wet. It's just not as cold as ice that you would make in your freezer.

That said, it is what I expected and is working really well so far. One of the faster of its type.

Update: It's a year and a few months later, and this ice maker is STILL working great. We make gallon baggies of ice during the day and drop them into the freezer on our sailboat. Later we have more than enough ice to last us through an evening of sundowners.

persona: This customer values accurate product specifications and reliable compatibility, prioritizing ease of installation and functional integrity over price, and tends to trust sellers who provide clear instructions and ensure parts fit correctly without modification.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Perfect match, fired right up Was tough finding a stove igniter for my 7 year old Kenmore range, so I was thankful this seller carried a compatible part. Identical size, no splicing necessary. Wire connections are keyed by connector size, so you can't reverse them. Nothing difficult about the fix. The included, printed disclaimer is understandable...the seller can't risk liability for a dufus who doesn't know simple DIY fixes. Nearly anyone can do the repair. Just unplug the range, turn off the gas valve before the range, and you're good to go. Mine fired right up after reassembling the fireplate cover, turning gas valve back on, plugging in range. Hope product has durability and lasts awhile.

rating 5.0. Seems to work fine. I have a Samsung Showcase RH25H5611SR/AA. It is four years old.

This is how I fixed my problem, you might have a different problem.

It is a side-by-side that has a freezer on one side, and the refrigerator on the other.

The freezer worked fine, and the refrigerator was not very cold at all at the top few shelves. Noticed the milk went bad early. Checked with a thermometer and it was like 52 degrees up there.

Took the shelves and drawers out, and removed the cover over the condenser after removing all the screws and using a blow dryer to ease the ice build-up over the entire panel. Careful, there are power connectors to disconnect first in the upper left - tight fit. With the wires still connected, you will only get approximately 45 degrees of movement forward.

The plastic light covers on either side pop off and have a screw behind them. Just work on it with a blow dryer and it should eventually come off. Don't try to force it off. If it still won't come off, check and make sure you don't have any extra screws still securing the panel to the refrigerator.

On the back of the panel you removed, you will see the fan assembly. Opened the black case for the electric motor attached to the fan. It has little snappy things like a lot of cases do. It had water inside it. I then disconnected the electrical connector from the motor.

The fan assembly, which includes a grey plastic housing, is connected by four screws to the back of the main body of the panel you removed from inside the refrigerator.

The motor shaft extends through the plastic fan to the other side, where it is attached with a weak green epoxy, and for lack of a better term, a metal coil around the outside of the plastic. I removed the green epoxy with a dental pick, and used some pliers to gently twist the metal coil off in the opposite direction of how it is coiled.

The plastic where the motor shaft meets with the fan has three points - think of a circle with three notches cut out.

I just took an appropriately sized center punch and pushed on the shaft with one hand, and gently lifted the plastic fan with the other until the fan came off the shaft. The motor will now freely come out of the other side.

The plastic housing the fan moves in (against? Near. Near is good.) has two concentric rings, I put a small amount of white lithium grease where the fan 'could' meet with the housing and rotated the fan against it to spread. This step is not required, and probably not recommended, but I did it anyway.

I put the new motor in the black housing and closed it. I put the fan on the motor shaft to approximate where it was on the old one and replaced the metal coil by gently twisting it into place with a pair of pliers. I did not replace the epoxy, but you might want to put a dab of loctite or something on the end. I figure that if this motor failure is a design flaw and it happens again, I don't want to deal with the epoxy next time.

I tilted the fan to the orientation it will be inside the refrigerator to test it - it needs to rotate freely, so don't jam it down flush with the housing, that's not how it was originally.

After that check, I reconnected the fan housing to the body of the panel.

I then placed the panel back in the refrigerator, and reconnected the power connectors on the upper left.

Replaced the screws and light covers. Replaced drawers and put a thermometer inside on the top shelf.

The new motor fixed the problem I was experiencing. The refrigerator was appropriately cold again.

Huzzah.

It developed a different problem after repair, However. The water from the dispenser would not come out.

What had happened is the bottom of the refrigerator got so cold that it froze the water in the filter. I removed the filter and saw ice crystals. I ran the filter under hot water and dumped it out until the water that came back out was not ice cold.

I don't know if using hot water is bad, you can use cold water if you want, but I was tired of dealing with it all at this point so I used hot water and when I replaced my filter, it worked just fine.

Hope this was helpful.

2 person install reccomended, one to push the dryer in, the oth... Review 18: rating 5.0. Whirlpool dryer vent It moved my dryer much closer to the wall. (Small Utility Rm) You do need a sheet metal cutter and sheet metal tape to seal after the cut in order to fit it in.

persona: This customer values reliable, purpose-built products that deliver consistent performance, even if they come at a higher price, and is willing to pay more for functionality and durability over cheaper, generic alternatives.
reference_output:
rating 4.0. Not idiot proof Installation is not necessarily idiot proof. Exhibit A: Me. The periscope would not easily fit over the tailpipe extending out from the wall, so I opened the periscope so I could apply direct pressure to push it on. I didn't notice how the baffles were oriented before they fell out. I assumed the baffles were to be fully contained inside the periscope. The 1994 dryer overheated causing consequential damage which I repaired. I discovered that the baffles had slipped out of place and blocked the airflow (a few others have had the same issue). I then realized that the right angle bend of the baffles needs to extend outside of the periscope so that it will be captured in place when the periscope is slid back together. See the sequence of four photos that make my point. That is my current theory, and I am sticking with it unless/until I am proved an idiot for a second time.
Otherwise, the periscope served my needs of reducing the amount of space needed between the side wall of my laundry bay where the house dryer vent comes out, and the side of the dryer. I kept the periscope fully extended and leaning backwards against the back wall so that I can reach over the dryer and unscrew the dryer attached semirigid vent tubing from the periscope with relative ease in the future.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. No instructions, no hardware, no Whirlpool/UL stamps on device I have not attempted to install the timer device I received. 1) It arrived with no instructions but this device must be configured. It can replace several different timers.
2) Most internet pictures show the timer shipping with mounting hardware. None wa... Review 2: rating 2.0. Not as pictured, mounting holes too small. Jumper was already configured to pin #1 (which was correct for my model after an internet search for the instructions) but I had to drill the holes to the proper size to fit the plastic pins in the frig. Working for now but a real pain. The genuine Whirlpool FSP replacement kit includes mounting hardware and installat... Review 3: rating 5.0. Timer Works great, came on time and price was super conpared to other places. You can always count on amazon to get you a great price and service Review 5: rating 1.0. Not Genuine Whirlpool part Sold by Supply Edge and fulfilled by Amazon. Listed as a genuine Whirlpool part. I was a Whirlpool parts distributor for 40 plus years. This is a non genuine part. Comes in plain box, no identification of mfg or brand. No mounting hardware and no instructions included. The timer has a loose lead you have to install on terminal 1 or 2. You must have the instructions to know which terminal to place lead on. Different. Terminal used for certain models. I ord... Review 6: rating 2.0. Part only not a kit This is the only a part and does not contain any instructions or any of the screws you may need for your particular installation. Review 7: rating 4.0. Defrosts every 24 hours so after 2 days of no defrost I finally got the 3rd try correct. Oh BTW I work in electronics for a living. Definitely if self installing, have patience or a degree in EE. Gave 1 star for shipping and full paper, mounting hardwares but I could not get it working. Applied correcting but heater will not turn off once engaged, lost a fridge full of food! Review 12: rating 5.0. Does what it should. Works great. Make sure to follow the directions when wiring, this part replaces several different type timers. It does run the defrost cycle more often than the old one, the original was a 12 hr continuous run, this unit defrosts every 8hr when wired continuous run, I view this as a plus.

Mounting hardware and instructions were in the box. Review 13: rating 4.0. Good luck. I thought I had to buy another ... It works. Did not come with screws or instructions. I thought I had to buy another frig. So far so good Review 14: rating 3.0. ... the original with the same part number but functions fine. The only problem is the mounting holes are ... This is a little smaller that the original with the same part number but functions fine. The only problem is the mounting holes are much larger and the original self tapping screws did not work. It was PITA as I did not have a selection of screws in my truck and attempts to insert wood or other fillers in the holes to get the original screws to grip, did not work to . You can... Review 15: rating 5.0. and everything works like new. The holes for the screws aren't the ... My freezer was really frosted up due to a long term problem. I scraped snow off the roof of the freezer several times after the repair . It's been three weeks, the snow is gone, and everything works like new.

The holes for the screws aren't the same, but there's no need to use the screws. Review 16: rating 1.0. One Star Was not the part we expected. Review 17: rating 1.0. Would not buy again, at least not from this seller, unless packaging is changed. Review 18: rating 1.0. One Star Would not function at all. Trying to find a different vendor. Review 19: rating 1.0. But no instructions were included, so it's up to you to search the web and try and find them. Very annoying. Probably not a good choice unless you have the wiring schematic for your refrigerator, and can read it.

Review 1: rating 5.0. EXCELLENT fit IF your washer has this wobbling issue it might just work. JUST keep in mind that in some cases the tubs will be completely off balance so badly (as in my case) and we still have not identified the issue. This part is an inexpensive way to help diagnose your washer's wobbling issue though. Review 2: rating 5.0. perfect fit and tension This is the original Whirlpool part and has the exact fit and tension to level the washer drum just right... Review 3: rating 4.0. FIXED MY WHIRLPOOL WASHER Boy my washer was unbalanced. I looked up on You Tube how to re-balance it and it comes down to 3 springs. Two side springs and the all important back Suspension Spring. Not brain surgery. My suspension spring had rusted at the bottom. I tried to take the rusted part off and re-bend what's left but that leaves the long part of the spring a tad short, and still unbalanced! This inexpensive spring was cheaper buying it on Amazon and figuring it out myself ... Review 4: rating 5.0. Works great, genuine part and fixed the wobbling spin cycle problem! Anyone with a compatible washer that's having the tub wobbling around like crazy during the spin cycle and can narrow it down to this issue- this is your part. Would highly recommend changing the other springs at the same time, just to make sure you're using a new set; but it works just fine as is.

Came WELL before the estimated arrival date and would definitely re-order if it ever breaks again.
If your machine has extended years on it, I'd also consider changing the three lateral springs on the bottom of tub to improve... Review 15: rating 5.0. Sweet! Works well, and now I don't have to replace my previously possessed washer because it bangs the drum against the cover. WIN! Review 16: rating 5.0. Five Stars Worked Great and was easy to install. Review 17: rating 5.0. works as intended works as designed. fixed my whirlpool washer shake. Review 18: rating 5.0. Good replacement spring Replaced on washing machine and it works great. Looks like the one I replaced. Review 19: rating 5.0. ALWAYS REPLACE ALL SPRINGS-NVR JUST THE ONE! BUY TWO- DONT BE A FOO! Review 20: rating 5.0. Easy fix Exactly what I needed. I have a 20 yr whirlpool so I wasn't sure if this was the right part, I opened the washer first and after taking the old spring out and making sure I bought it. Nothing too complicated, the hardest part was putting cover back on the washer. Very well packed too.

Review 19: rating 5.0. much better buy than buying individually!! Buying this product in a combo set is very convenient... All the parts came individually wrapped and labeled with its part number... It didn't take long for me to install them and have my dryer getting it back to work again... You save a lot of money buying this combo pack. You will spend close to $90 at a local appliance store buying the individual pieces Review 20: rating 5.0. Repairing a dryer is not hard In general, repairing a dryer is not hard. This kit gives you practically everything you need to repair most problems related to a dryer that turns but doesn't heat up. In my case, the problem was the heating element itself. I saved the three other switches in case they fail in the future. I didn't even have to remove the dryer. I just tipped it on its side and removed the back.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Excellent Item Finally, a truly easy way to fill re-useable K-cup containers.  No counter mess, no fuss....1-2-3 done.  Couldn't be better.  Can't understand how anyone could have problems with this device.

Review 2: rating 4.0. Four Stars I do like this, however it overfills the pod if filled more than 3/4 full.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Genius! This is an awesome gadget.  We wanted to save money by using the reusable pods, but know how messy that can sometimes be.  We bought this, along with the cute little filters.  After placing the filter in the pod, I use this to flawlessly fill the cup, then turn the scoop around and use the back of it to press the coffee and filter into the pod.  Perfect every time!

Review 4: rating 5.0. Five Stars Perfect size and very easy to use, even for someone with severe arthritis in the fingers.

Review 5: rating 5.0. This little scoop makes filling a K-cup pod extremely easy. When I first ordered some refillable K-cups I ... This little scoop makes filling a K-cup pod extremely easy.  When I first ordered some refillable K-cups I though I might end-up finding them too difficult to use and abandon my going green intentions but this scoop makes it so easy I have had no trouble sticking with the reusable cups.  I found another advantage too...I like a bold cup of coffee and with this scoop and the r...

Review 6: rating 3.0. Good Idea But Has a Few Issues This is an example of a good idea that still needs a few tweaks to be fully realized. I use the My K-Cup Reusable Filter, and have been looking for a funnel-type tool to be able to get the coffee into the tiny capsule. I thought this was the answer, and it's close, but not quite there. It has a scoop on one side and a funnel part that flips over on the other side. Once you've scooped the coffee, you're to flip it over and use the funnel to pour the coff...

Review 7: rating 2.0. filler just big and unhandy to use

Review 8: rating 2.0. Looked like a good idea. This is a good idea, but it's too big. Makes a mess worse than using a normal spoon or coffee scoop. I just don't like it.

Review 9: rating 3.0. Three Stars Great idea, but I still spill coffee everywhere.

Review 10: rating 2.0. No great advantage No great advantage over any other measuring device.  But it did not spill and was easy to use.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Works great! In an effort to save $$ and not use unrecyclable k-cups, we decided to use the generic refillable cups. I've been filling them with a spoon--what a mess! I tried using a teaspoon--requiring more than one spoon full, and a tablespoon. In both cases, coffee grounds went everywhere. This little gadget works great! Easy to use; you can control how much you fill the cup just by controlling how much you're scooping then, with a flip, you easily dump it into the empty k-cup. An...

Review 12: rating 5.0. EZ scoop (perfect pod brand) is very nice.. EZ scoop (perfect pod brand) is very nice....no mess, funnels the coffee right in.

Review 13: rating 2.0. Clumsey to dip in coffee Kind of bulkey good concept but I dont really use mine.  Nice to pour in k cup basket though.  Don' know if I'd call it perfect.

Review 14: rating 5.0. makes it easier to get a uniform amount of coffee in the refillable cups had one to refill my Keurig refillable cups. My son liked it so much I order him one as well.

Review 15: rating 2.0. Great idea, but we find it much easier to ... Great idea, but we find it much easier to just use a teaspoon to fill out kcup pods. This was more difficult to use.

Review 16: rating 5.0. easy way to fill reusable k-cups Quick, no mess, easy way to fill reusable k-cups. Great idea!

Review 17: rating 5.0. Does what it's designed to do, brilliant idea! What is not to like about this?  It makes it so much easier to put grounds in the K-cup.  The first attempt, i filled the scoop with grounds, used my thumb to hold it down, turned it over, and poured the grounds into the K-cup.  Granted, some grounds came out the top of the K-cup.  The seond time, I didn't use as many grounds and guess what, no spillage!  My husband shakes a little, has chondral tunnel in both elbows, left handed, and wa...

Review 18: rating 5.0. Nifty gadget The product arrived on time and in perfect condition. This does what it says - easily refill coffee k-cup/capsules - once you remember to hold both parts together before turning to fill the cup!  We're in the over 65 age group and tend to have slight unsteadiness and tremors so this is very helpful in reducing the mess of coffee grounds all over the counter and floor.

Review 19: rating 4.0. Works It works.  You just need to get use to it.  Holds too much for a ez cup but you can still use it.

Review 20: rating 3.0. Great idea, but one design flaw Nice idea, with one design flaw.  Bought it to fill the k-cup without spilling coffee grounds, however, there is a groove in the handle the same side as the fill cup, and coffee gets caught isn't here, so when I turn it to dump it into the k-cup, the coffee that got caught in the groove in the handle still spills out into the counter.

Not an appliance I'd want people to see.

I'm glad I was in front of it to catch it. Review 8: rating 1.0. Banged up Refrigerator Huge dent on the top and scratches. Worst purchase ever from Amazon. Don't buy it. Review 9: rating 5.0. Arrived in perfect condition, via an Amazon freight truck with two handlers. We ordered on a weekend, so it shipped when UPS isn't working (mindful of the reviews that said nearly all UPS arrived damaged). Our big fridge di... Review 10: rating 3.0. Retro Fridge Fridge delivered upside down. Doesn’t level correctly due to bent parts. Review 11: rating 5.0. Awesome Don’t kno if I just got lucky or what but they seem to have definitely improved their packaging as I didn’t have any issues an the fridge is a perfect replacement to a regular fridge in emergency Review 12: rating 3.0. Not bad, yet flawed! Ordered and received this frig very quickly. Gave it a full 24hrs+ before plugging it in as I wanted coolant to settle after shipping. First thing I noticed on the unboxing was that the RC a logo was crooked. I plugged the fridge in and waited to see how long it was going to be before everything started to cooling and the main area and then of course the freezer. it took well over 9 hours before the main fridge area to get cool but the freezer got cool with in 2... Review 13: rating 5.0. Remarkable, very pleased. Quite a remarkable small refrigerator, very pleased.
The refrigerator arrived undamaged Hurrah. I read other reviews that had commented on shipping damage however thought I'd give it a go and to my delight not so much as a small dent or scratch.
The interior of the refrigerator and freezer compartments are surprisingly large.
I choose the black and it blends in beautifully in my small country kitchen.
The size is sensible for a single per... Review 14: rating 5.0. Awesome refrigerator... Since I got this fridge 6 months ago...I love it. Not yet to have no frost at all. So quiet I thought it was broken when I got first day. That's how quiet is. Perfect for me. I'm SS I live in a small apt. You would not believe the size for freezer and fridge. 10 stars for this one Review 15: rating 1.0. Badly damaged in shipping Like the size of it but it was badly damaged from shipping . One corner was completely crushed not dented. There were 4 dents on the front and the bottom left was dented in. Sooo I am not too happy with it 😒 Review 16: rating 5.0. Small fridge/freezer The fridge works well but it came with a small dent. When I called customer service he was very helpful and gave me a discount back to my account. Very pleased! Review 17: rating 5.0. Overall it is good, a little regret. The refrigerator is delivered quickly, I like it very much, but there is a hole in the packing box, and there is a collision mark on the back and the front of the refrigerator. It's a shame that it's not perfect Review 18: rating 1.0. Bad fridge The fridge arrived not working, compressor would not turn on. Large and useless lump of metal. It was also damaged in several areas, including the freezer door hinge, and would not shut correctly. Extremely disappointed. Review 19: rating 3.0. Don’t pay full price for a dented fridge! If you’re going to order a fridge and have it shipped, you will get dents. This is just a heads up that it is typical of delivery drivers to handle these larger and heavier packages much more roughly than regular ones. Mine came with the top back left corner smushed a bit, dents in the back, left and right sides, as well as a broken plastic lining in the bottom. Luckily the shelves were taped down well enough that it came intact with no broke... Review 20: rating 5.0. Great fridge. I absolutely love this refridgerator. It is very quiet and the freezer keeps food frozen. It looks good too.
persona: This customer values functional, space-saving appliances that are easy to install, operate efficiently, and integrate seamlessly into compact living spaces, with a preference for products that deliver strong performance and convenience without requiring major kitchen renovations.
reference_output: rating 5.0. I should have bought this earlier! It's great! I was able to easily lift this refrigerator into my house, remove the packaging, and level it all in about 30 minutes. I read and followed the installation instructions, and in a few hours had everything running beautifully! The temperature control is excellent. I was able to put everything I routinely use in this refrigerator, and have plenty of room left over. The freezer is awesomely cold and doing a great job as well. This is the best money I have spent in quite some time.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 2.0. Poor quality / look cheap These look and feel cheap. They're basically a flimsy metal tin. Not worth the $15 paid or to pay for return shipping. Review 2: rating 1.0. Waste of money It didn't fit. I returned it. It's very cheaply made. It's only meant to cover up dirty stove tops. My initial review had 2 stars, but now I've changed it to 1 star because I am Very unhappy with Amazon and this vendor right now. Review 3: rating 1.0. Black Marple Burner Covers Very disappointed. Very cheap made. Review 4: rating 1.0. The product dimensions must be for the very outer edge ... The product dimensions must be for the very outer edge and not the inside where it would fit over the burner. Consequently, won't fit and I had to pay to ship it back. The dimensions should note how it's measured or say for size burner it would fit. Review 5: rating 5.0. Beautiful covers to hide ugly burner. I love my new covers, they match my kichen colors. They hide my ugly burners. Review 6: rating 2.0. Two Stars They look good just not heavy enough Review 7: rating 2.0. Not what I expected Not what I expected I thought they were for gas burners but they are too thin so I gas they are for the electric burners. The first time my package arrived it was empty but the customer service was great and sent another set right out. Which is why I didn`t return the ones I have. So I am still looking for the gas stove covers. Review 8: rating 5.0. Works to cover electric burners as well! I loveeee these! Was worried that they would only work for gas stoves but they wonderful work on electric as well. Review 9: rating 5.0. Looks good and keeps crud off the drip pans Got these because an enthusiastic neighbor keeps knocking grit off the ceiling onto the stove, where it enamels onto the drip pans. Much nicer looking than I expected and covers up well. Bought for a random apartment stove and it fits with enough space in between for a spoon holder. Solid black on the underside. Doesn't make a hot element safe if you put it over a recently used burner (wouldn't expect it to), but it hasn't blistered or warp... Review 10: rating 5.0. These are truly helpful for my situation! I moved into a senior apartment, and it is a brand new building. Every time it gets windy outside, particles fall onto stove top from the exhaust fan. This makes cleaning it up so much easier! Wish they were just a tiny bit deeper, but am still very happy. I have an electric stove with the coils. Just remember 'not' to turn a burner on with these in place! Review 11: rating 1.0. Not deep enough These are not deep enough for my gas stove Review 12: rating 4.0. Work good for splatter cover I like to keep one of these on the side of the stove I am not using to keep all the grease & splatter from getting on the burners. I have a gas stove & the burners are a pain to clean when others cook & don't clean up right away. These work great because I can wash them quickly enough in the sink. The only thing I would like better is if they were more durable. They are pretty flimsy. When washing, they twist easily. I assume they are made more as a decor... Review 13: rating 1.0. DISAPPOINTMENT LIKE:: THEY ARE ATTRACTIVE & KEEP THE EYES CLEANER.

DISLIKE: THEY ARE FLEXIBLE TIN, VERY LIGHT IN WEIGHT, NOT AS STRONG AS I WOULD LIKE. Review 14: rating 1.0. Not marble. Not at all what I expected. Pretty thin and bend easily. The color hides any imperfections Review 17: rating 4.0. Very slight...don't put cast iron pans on them Electric burners are unsightly...these covers Jaz up the look of the kitchen Review 18: rating 1.0. Eh Didn't withstand residual heat. Dropped once and bent. Didn't fit stove probably over burners Review 19: rating 1.0. No bueno Too big, doesn't fit on stovetop.
Was bent on side when arrived. Review 20: rating 2.0. Like cheap sheet pans Very cheap. Like cheap sheet pans, with some spray paint to make them look marble.
