I still like the variety packs of random coffees on occasion, but I do have my favorites, which I can now buy in bulk to save money, and make without feeling like I'm just creating ... Review 12: rating 5.0. Great product! I was little skeptical about this product. I have been experimenting from the tip sheet that came with the (3)Ekobrew cups I ordered. The tips are right on the money. Fill the Ekocup 3/4 for a finer grind, and fill it up for a coarser grind. I think the water drips through the Ekobrew cup better with the coarser grind(Like classic Folgers). I get a nice, full-bodied cup of coffee. I plan to buy Dunkin Donuts Coffee whole beans, and coarsely grind them at the store. I ... Review 13: rating 2.0. Meh....... The "micro-mesh" screen on the Ekobrew is a larger mesh size compared to the Solofil. This lets a lot more fine particles through leaving a silty mess at the bottom of each cup. Mine also leaks around seal; I can tell the lid doesn't fit as tight as on the Solofil.

I bought this hoping I could make a stronger cup of coffee because it holds more grounds than the Solofil, but the two issues above negate that benefit.

Back to the Solofil....... Review 14: rating 5.0. Perfect coffee! After buying the Keurig brand K-cup filter I was a little disappointed because it was a pain to clean out each time and it used more coffee grounds than I expected. When I began shopping for a different kind I came across this one and I'm glad I gave it a try! It uses less coffee than the Keurig brand while still making the same strength & flavored cup of coffee. It also is much easier to clean the used coffee grounds out to wash it. I will be buying more for myse... Review 15: rating 5.0. Better than the My K-Cup I've been using The ekobrew Cup is an excellent replacement for the My K-Cup insert I've been using. Why?

1. It doesn't require removing the insert from the Keurig B60 I've got. So, it's quick. Also, there's no extra fiddling with the machine, which always makes me nervous.

2. It doesn't leak like the My K-Cup. I don't know if it was user error or what, but I had about given up on using my own coffee in the brewer. Every time, I had wate... Review 16: rating 5.0. Great Filter This filter is much better than the My K-Cup reusable filter. It is easier to use - no removing the machine's sleeve, easier to clean, and best of all, doesn't leak like the My K-cup filter did. The cover hinge does look a little flimsy but after a month of use, there is no sign that it has weakened. Plus, if it does break, it doesn't appear that this would keep the filter from working. Review 17: rating 2.0. B30 Won't Close Properly The design looks OK, but my D30 will not close easily with the insert in place. Buyer beware, they may have some quality control issues. I can only speak to my experience... Review 18: rating 5.0. Very happy with purchase Saving money by not using as much coffee as what is required for the original personal KCup filter. Review 19: rating 5.0. eco-friendly The only throw away waste is the bio-degradable grounds used in the filter. Review 20: rating 3.0. OK not great This item works much better than the standard one from Keurig but I was still disappointed. The water goes through too fast and the resulting coffee is not nearly as good (strong) as the K-cups. It's also a pain to clean out for each cup. But it does work and it is less expensive to use than the K-cups. Just not as convenient.
persona: This customer prioritizes ease of use, durability, and performance in coffee pod systems, preferring refillable options that outperform original equipment and simplify maintenance.
reference_output: rating 5.0. I Like It This Kcup refillable works great.  Easy to clean, easy to take in and out of machine.  It is definitely much better than the one that came with my Keurig.

No longer do I have to remind them to turn on the fan (or turn it off!) it does it automatically.
I held off one star because I found the installation instructions lacking. With this switch, you are required to tie it to a neutral wire. My j-box had a neutral shared with a light switch, that was buried in the back. I almost gave up when I couldn't find it. Lesson learned.The manufacturer swapped in a replacement which had a much quieter buzzing sound (barely barely audible). Can't even hear it unless you put your ear directly to it and listen very very carefully, which I don't plan to do during regular use of my bathroom fan. Humidity & timer functions still work well.

5 Stars for a great product and great product support from the manufacturer.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Works Great I waited a month to write this review.

Have used this little gem almost daily.

It cleans well, and is easy to use. You can NOT use it like a regular washer. These type of units are designed to be used with supervision.

Although it comes with fill hose I use my shower head. Set it in my shower for complete cycle.

It's true, the spin cycle is not great. Doesn't really extract enough water and it's hard to seat the spin basket. I don't use it, this was strictly purchased to wash as I have a great spinner.

It does the job and I'm very happy with it.

persona: This customer prioritizes authenticity and compatibility over cost, seeking genuine OEM parts that are reliable and easy to install, and is willing to pay for precision and performance to avoid frustration and repeated failures.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Aftermarket Belt Didn’t Work! This Did! So this was my 1st time changing a GE Dryer Belt.. there were only 2 stores in town with GE Dryer belts for my model but they were aftermarket (not like this one).  The sales guys assured me it was the same...it was NOT!! At first, I thought I was having difficulty because it was my 1st belt install, but after talking to the parts guy in town he told me that GE Dryers are known for having belts that are difficult to install!! Also, after ordering this belt I noticed it was easier to install the belt because the aftermarket belt I bought it town must have been different/shorter in length! So just a head’s up if you are having trouble it might not be you or your fault!  These belts don’t stretch so go with the belt from GE(this one)...and NOTan aftermarket belt!

Review 2: rating 5.0. Perfect fit and super fast shipping! This was a perfect fit for my refrigerator and bolted right in with no issues. It came with everything I needed to get the job done. The shipping was super fast from this company! I will definitely buy from this seller again in the future.

It may be a bit louder then the original part but that's OK. It came with screws, wire connectors, and rubber bases/seats for the blades. It doesn't come with the blades so hang on to your old one.

It should be noted that the polarity of the wires is not important...

reference_output: rating 5.0. Direct (but not exact) replacement for Maytag PTB2154GRW This was an easy-enough replacement for my fridge:  Maytag PTB2154GRW from 2003.  Original motor was a GE 5KSM51AG.  There was a 3-pin connector going to that motor.  I cut off the connector and wired the two black wires from this motor to the orange and the blue wires bringing power in to old motor.  I connected the ground (green/yellow stripe) wire to the body of the new fan where it attaches to one of the mounting arms.  Easiest method was to unscrew the back and bottom screws where motor attaches to arms, then twist those arms out of the way and lift the motor and top arm out of its rubber mounting grommet.  To reinstall, move fan blades to new motor and attach new fan to the top arm; slide it into the top rubber mounting grommet, then replace the other two screws.  I had to use the screws that came with the new fan (different thread pitch).  I knew the old one needed replaced because the area between the doors has been hot to the touch for quite a while--I didn't even realize I hadn't heard the old fan running (probably for close to a year--ouch.  It's a testament to this old fridge that it's still working.  If you can fix the old one cheap, don't buy a new one that will only last one third as long.)  I rolled the fridge out to clean it and realized the compressor was running but the fan was not--it started when I spun it by hand, but it was going much slower than it should have been.  This was a good investment for someone with any basic DIY skill.  Don't forget to unplug the fridge before you start this repair.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Filter tears I've used mine 4 times so far and each time the filter busts and grounds end up in my coffee. Review 2: rating 3.0. but it's far from Perfect. It's ok in that it's not rejected by the Keurig. But the brew is a bit lackluster, even when choosing strong. I suspect it's the design of the cup. Unlike the patented K-cup with the small single bottom hole, this pod's bottom is wide open. Upshot? The slower flow of the brew in the K-Cup allows for a greater flavor enhancement. So until Keurig re-releases their My-Cup accessory this Fall, this Pod works, but it's far from Perfect. Review 3: rating 2.0. My Keurig broke shortly after I used this. The ... My Keurig broke shortly after I used this. The Keurig company replaced the brewer and said that these EZ cups most likely caused the damage. Review 4: rating 3.0. Grounds in your teeth This doesn't work as well as the one for the keurig one. The original one worked great...no grounds etc. Than I bought the keurig 2.0 and I get grounds in every one of my cups. I'm not sure if it's the ez cup or the machine but I'm not happy with the results. Review 5: rating 2.0. Easy to spill In trying to fill, snap the lid I've dumped it numerous times. Also I hoped that by being able to use the paper filters that I would not have the settlings / residue similar to using the fine wire mesh filters. This product has helped a bit with that issue but still no where near as when I use a traditional coffee maker. Review 6: rating 4.0. Best One Yet Best one I've tried yet. It's easy to remove the filter with a push from the bottom spring mechanism. You don't have to touch the filter and risk it tearing and spilling the grinds. The only problem is that over time, the lid starts pushing up and opens sometimes causing the grinds to spill and make a mess. To avoid this, you have to be careful not to overfill the grinds and leave room for the compression from the spring mechanism. To much pressure from overfilling ... Review 7: rating 3.0. Great for diner style weak side coffee, but not for a strong robust cup of coffee. It's great if you like your coffee on the weak side. I like my coffee on the strong side and robust, so this doesn't work for me. There's a fine line, not marked on the capsule, between weak coffee and a blown out filter that sends 75% of the grounds into your cup. A great design for drinking diner style coffee. Leaves me starving for a stronger cup of coffee and cleaning up the mess.

6/... Review 8: rating 2.0. I have been a happy user of the Perfect Pod for 1 I have been a happy user of the Perfect Pod for 1.0 Kureg coffee makers (3 of them) for many years so when I got my new Kurig 2.0 I ordered the new 2.0 Ez Cup from Amazon. Well, it doesn't work very well. its harder to get the lid in place which I can live with but the cup jams in the machine so I must remove the lid, them pull out the paper filter without getting coffee grounds all over the place then pry out the bottom piece. I s... Review 9: rating 1.0. Doesn’t fit in our GE Cafe fridge Keurig machine Does not fit in our Keurig machine. It is part of our GE Cafe refrigerator Keurig machine and this cup does not fit. Might be good to specify. Review 10: rating 1.0. Didn't like it The coffee overflows and put grounds in the cup plus it's very hard to push down Review 11: rating 1.0. Get mesh type not this Lets coffee grounds out into your coffee Review 12: rating 5.0. Works great The paper liners make cleanup quick and easy. I'm thankful this does actually work with the Keurig 2.0 as advertised. Review 13: rating 5.0. Five Stars Awsome and easy to use. Review 14: rating 2.0. Two Stars too expensive for what I recieved...must use a paper filter also, gets even more expensive at time goes by. Review 15: rating 1.0. One Star This did not even work in my Keurig 2.0 I had to buy one from Keurig direct. Review 16: rating 2.0. Two Stars It doesn't work with the Keurig 2.0 Review 17: rating 2.0. Doesn't Fit It doesn't fit my Keurig. Review 18: rating 1.0. Quit working. worked great for about 5 times, now 2.0 gives OOPs code and can't read pod. very disappointing. . Review 19: rating 1.0. Coffee tastes like colored water The filter paper is too thick. Coffee tastes like colored water! Awful product. I have experimented with different grinds and even leaving paper lid off. Nope Review 20: rating 1.0. It does work, but very disappointing The EZ Cup does work in our Keurig 2.0. However, it is very difficult to use. There is a spring in the bottom of the cup, you have to balance the paper filter on it, and manage to put in your coffee without making a mess. The you have to push the spring down with the paper filter in order to put the lid on the cup. It's very cumbersome & time consuming.
persona: This customer values convenience, cleanliness, and ease of use in coffee preparation, preferring products that minimize mess and effort while enabling customization with their own coffee, and is likely to repurchase items that streamline their brewing routine.
reference_output: rating 4.0. These little EZ cup pods are GREAT! If you want to use your own coffee, this is the way to go and I LOVE the little filters, because once you put them in the pod and fill them with coffee, put on the lid and fix he coffee, all you do when finished (after it cools down) is remove the filter from the pod and you'll find you've actually made your own little coffee pod in the process that you can just take it out of the EZ cup and pitch it in the garbage. NO more digging, washing, knocking the coffee grounds out of the EX cups! No mess. Just pull out the filter and toss it in the garbage! I alternate between ready-made pods and using my own coffee, so this is the best of both worlds! I just got the filters and I am amazed at how great they work and how easy they are to use. Another thing, I don't particularly care for all the plastic in the purple perfect pod EZ cup, so I am using the filters in my Perfect Pod ECO-flow stainless steel cup and they work great. Cuts down on me getting a dose of plastic everytime I use the Perfect Pod EZ cup. The Perfect Pod ECO-FLO stainless steel  cup substitutes, in my opinion just as well! That is the only reason I gave the Perfect Pod EZ cup a 4 start instead of a 5. They need to make it out of stainless steel and get rid of the plastic!  In the meantime, the Perfect Pod ECO-FLO cup is already made of stainless steel, problem solved (for me)! So, now when I switch back & forth between using the compostable pods (the only ones I use since the others are full of plastic) and using my own coffee grounds, it is SO MUCH EASIER! No mess!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. One Star This ice maker was USED! Review 2: rating 5.0. Great replacement, works like NEW! My Whirlpool gold fridge was struggling to make ice and leaking water constantly. After reading through forums I found the best solution was to replace the ice maker assembly which I found here on Amazon for $10 cheaper than other appliance sites. Replacing this was pretty easy after I found some disassembly guides online. Was all plug and play. Works great now, getting constant full ice cubes no and no more leaking. Review 3: rating 5.0. Worked as described Fixed my ice issue. It was new, and had a sticker on it indicating that it was only made a few months ago. The install is something someone with a couple screw drivers and a 10 mm ratchet could accomplish. Just be careful pulling down the plastic shield/top cover. I followed this video to install it. Youtube DOT COM /watch?v=ZzXTVyrV5nU&t=540s Review 4: rating 5.0. It works! Not so easy to install, but if you find the right YouTube video you can do it. No special tools necessary. It just takes so many steps just to get to the ice maker. Once you get there it's drop it in, plug in the wires (and the water hose a few steps later). It just feels so good to swap in a new part and not pay for the service call visit. Oh and, the part works, it was a perfect fit/replacement. And my wife stopped complaining about no ice. Review 5: rating 1.0. Did the job... UPDATE- IT QUIT AFTER 6 MONTHS. Much cheaper to do yourself. Especially if you can do your own diagnostics on your refrigerator. Newer models have diagnostics codes if you read the manual. Review 6: rating 1.0. Does not work This did not work. We had it delivered and waited to put it in. Put it in this past week and tried everything to get it to work. Had to purchase another one and it worked. Got into return it and says that I can no longer return. Return date closed 4 days ago! Review 7: rating 2.0. First one lasted 1.5 years I just purchased my 2nd one from prime. Prime has the best price on these. My rating is for the Whirlpool icemaker not for Prime. Review 8: rating 1.0. Very expensive paperweight. First of all, the ice maker arrived banging around in a box. There was absolutely no packing at all, no paperwork, nothing (see pic). So, I guess it’s no wonder that it was defective. Installation is as easy as eating lettuce, there are several YouTube videos that’ll walk you they it. Upon finishing installation, it immediately failed the diagnostic...got a “E2” code, motor lost position. Anyway, it‘ll Bree promptly returned. Review 9: rating 4.0. Whirlpool Gold Refrigerator Received the product without delay. The ice maker came loose in a small cardboard box with no packing material as others have commented. Installed it in about 20 minutes after finding a video on YouTube. Ice appeared a short time after installation. Appears to be working fine. Will update later if anything changes. Subtracted a star due to the way it was shipped ( without packing material). Otherwise, I would buy again. Review 10: rating 1.0. Original ice maker was defective Original Ice maker ordered made one tray of ice and quit. I was stumped since the codes called for a new ice maker mechanism and that didn't fix the problem. I called out a technician who told me that the new part was no good. Your company was good about the exchange and the replacement works fine. BUT IT COST ME $97.00!!!!! because the original part I bought was no good! Review 11: rating 1.0. Horrible customer service Didnt work...whirlpool doesnt give a crap. I wish when I called customer support I could speak to someone with the slightest bit of knowledge! Review 12: rating 1.0. Look out! Bought 1-8-20 installed 1-9-20, UNIT FAILED, 02-18-20. Return/refund date passed 02-09, dam.
BE WARNED! Review 13: rating 1.0. DEFECTIVE!!! DON’T BUY!!! Defective product! My first order arrived and it didn’t work. So I called a Whirlpool technician who confirmed after two hours that it was a defective unit. So I got a replacement, and after less than 3 months it stopped working too. Don’t buy here. Spend a few extra dollars and get it from a local source that warranties their products. This seller wouldn’t refund my money so I had to get a replacement and wouldn’t reimburse me for the service call. And the... Review 14: rating 1.0. When do i get credit for missing item. The box showed up empty. Nothing in it. Review 15: rating 5.0. Easily fixed my Whirlpool Refrigerator’s Ice Maker Product arrived a day early; it was a welcome surprise. installation was easy and only took about 40 minutes. After successfully running the diagnostics on my, Whirlpool refrigerator and putting the ice maker into the, “emergency ice” mode it started making ice within the hour. Review 16: rating 1.0. Part arrived defective Part arrived, looked like a used part. Went through all the trouble installing the “new” ice maker and it failed the testing right away. Part is defective and won’t make ice.

I don’t recommend buying from this source. You’re not getting a new part. Review 17: rating 2.0. Part did not work and had to be returned Ice maker did not work. Returned on warranty claim. Bit of a hassle. Money refunded Review 18: rating 2.0. Did not fix my problem It did not fix the problem, I still do not have ice; not sure if it needed a preplacement but I bought it based on the vidio I watched. Review 19: rating 1.0. Did not work. I replaced my ice maker with this one. However, I had let the return time expire before installing it. This ice maker would only make small cube not much larger that a thumbnail. I will have to buy another one. Review 20: rating 5.0. This went flawlessly I found the Amazon product page link from a YouTube video showing instruction on how to replace this. It worked so well. I followed the instructions and had the new ice maker installed within less than 30 minutes. It has been making ice without an issue since.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Run diagnostics first This is my 2nd ice maker. Replaced first due to diagnostic error on panel. Replaced this even though diagnostics were fine. reference_output: rating 5.0. The Heat of the Moment This is stamped as a real Whirlpool part.  Installs in seconds. The burner is hot and it supports a full range of temperatures based on your dial setting.  Nice build quality and a great price.

My refrigerator was getting warm one day and cold the next. This fixed the problem. Does take a few days to get the temperature correct, but works as it should. Review 9: rating 5.0. Works just fine. I purchased this thermostat to replace one that was no longer made. The difference was that the mounting brackets were different. Review 10: rating 1.0. Description and picture was inaccurate Part arrived late. When it did arrive, I realized it was the wrong part. You need this part if:
Your fridge doesn't start cooling automatically
It won't stop running
It won't start running
Its inconsistent in temp w/o changing dial

I bought it based on the pictures... a gamble. Worked out very well! Review 14: rating 2.0. Over the course of the next couple of weeks, I had to continually fiddle with the freezer and fridge temperature control knobs to try to find some balance where the freezer was cold enough without freezing the fridge. I used a thermometer in both sides to monitor, and this thing had temps all over the place. On a 2, my fridge side would be c... Review 15: rating 2.0. However, sooner rather than later you had to turn the cold control knob . Since the fridge is 15 years old thought it would be best to replace rather than repair it. Due to cabinet size restrictions most of the models we wanted were on back order for at least 3 months... Review 17: rating 1.0. beware item installed now fridge runs nonstop. Review 18: rating 1.0. Bought another thermostat (from a different supplier) and the world is right again. Review 19: rating 5.0. fixed the problem This thermostat was easy to install. Arrived promptly and well packaged. rating 1.0. Unfortunately, poor quality, not worth it I bought this as a replacement. Shocked at the price difference between this vs a "real" parts store. reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. It creates a false sense of security! The filter appears to be everything I would want from a system in my fridge, until you put the water to a test. A TDS meter measures the total dissolved solids in our water. Contaminants and minerals in our water that may be harmful are measured by the meter. The closer to zero when measured, the less contaminants are in the water. Truly clean water should measure at zero.

Once I received the filter and ran water through it for a week,... Review 2: rating 5.0. Installation Instructions This water filter will last around 6 months; and it definitely works! After about the fifth month, the water coming from the fridge tasted just like tap water; after a couple days with the new filter installed, my fridge's filtered water tasted more neutral/good again. Highly recommended!

** Installation Instructions **

Since it was difficult for me to find installation instructions online, and the instructions on the box worried me a l... Review 3: rating 5.0. easy to install Arrived on time, easy to install, original LG filter... Everything promised was delivered.

We went with this filter because a lot of the "off" brands are for taste only and this is a certified filter for contaminants and filters also for taste. We have never had a problem with an LG filter tasting funny or leaking. It is worth it for the few dollars difference. Review 4: rating 1.0. Description on item not accurate They do not fit the fridge listed on the description. Not compatable Review 5: rating 1.0. This filter is useless. My TDS from tap is about 290 ppm ... This filter is useless. My TDS from tap is about 290 ppm and with this filter it's about 240 ppm while my osmosis system is 15 ppm. I thought my old filter was getting dirty because it read similar reading before installing a new one I guess it's just the filter itself overall is useless. Returning and will connect my osmosis system to the frig instead....don't want to get myself killed. Review 6: rating 5.0. Good replacement filter for Kenmore I have a Kenmore refrigerator, I've been using this new filter for a week now and it's been great so far. Don't need to spend extra $$$ to get Kenmore (ADQ36006102 ) The LG (ADQ36006101 ) is the one to get unless Kenmore filters come down in price. Review 7: rating 2.0. Refrigerator Filter This water filter for the refrigerator was purchased in May 2020. It is August 21, 2020, 3 months and it has to be replaced. This filter should last 6 months. Review 8: rating 2.0. Scammed Good product but way overpriced! I could have purchased three of the same filters at the same price as this one filter! Review 9: rating 5.0. LG LT700P Refrigerator Water Filter This is an exact replacement for the Kenmore version. The Kenmore brand filter is 50% more at Sears for some reason... The replacement reminder on the fridge is set for a specific volume +/- and not the actual filter condition or flow, so if you have a decent water supply it can easily go through 2+ cycles without a drop in water quality.
I was apprehensive to replace the old filter, as it was the first time that it needed to be replaced. Inst... Review 10: rating 5.0. Beware of fakes! This one is a true genuine filter. I first bought from eBay a filter listed as "genuine" but got a fake (you can actually see typos on the box). Since then, I made sure I bought genuine ones sold by Amazon. I also tried the Home Depot brand but they only lasted a few months. I still prefer the taste with the OEM filter, and it lasts longer. Review 11: rating 2.0. Not as happy as in the past Not that happy with product. We've ordered them in the past and they worked fine but this time the first one didn't work and had to be replaced and now we're hearing a weird noise from the 2nd one. We've never had problems in the past. I feel like we got a bad batch or something. Review 12: rating 1.0. Does not fit properly! This product is advertised to replace an LG filter LT 700 P. This filter does not fit my refrigerator! Do not buy this product. Apparently the manufactures have not Tested this properly. The stem is too wide and too short. It does not mount properly! Review 13: rating 5.0. Buy the real thing not the generic one! This is exactly what it is supposed to be. I bought the generic one and learned my lesson. It only lasted half as long and the water shot out so violently that you got drenched each time you used it. I will only buy this one from now on. Easy to install and lasts exactly how long it is supposed to. Review 14: rating 1.0. it is not working well, I live in Texas ... it is not working well, I live in Texas where the faucet water is very hard(zero water reading 434). surprisingly after change the filter water meter read higher (476)number after I through away initial filtered water. well.. so now I buy drinking water --;; Review 15: rating 2.0. I was surprised! I had to give it 2 stars because it didn’t fit in my LG refrigerator even though it matched the model number and I had bought this before. I was disappointed that the size had changed and it would no longer fit. It was too long. I had to return it. Review 16: rating 1.0. not worth it It has not been even a month and the filter stopped working. Too expensive to get results like this. I would rather go with a different brand that works with my refrigerator. Review 17: rating 5.0. Strange Pricing It shipped fairly quickly. It went in easily. Product is great.

I was surprised that if you buy them in bulk you PAY MORE. I paid $34.02. If I would have gotten a two pack, they'd cost $38.75 each; same if I bought three. It was $38 had I bought a four pack and 37.50 if I bought six.

It's over $20 cheaper to buy six individual filters rather than the six pack.

Anyway, it's a good product. You should buy one...but only one.
<... Review 18: rating 1.0. Hmmmmmm Never worked, Amazon refunded my money. Review 19: rating 1.0. Can Taste Chlorine After installing a new filter per directions on the box, including flushing, water has distinct and strong taste of chlorine. Review 20: rating 1.0. Filter failed about one month after being installed. I purchased the filter in January and stored it in it's original box until put to use in early May. It's now mid June and the water comes out with significantly lower pressure. We tested the hypothesis that it was the filter by removing it and placing the bypass tube into the refrigerator. Less than two months in and we already need a new filter.

NX58R4311SS compatible Worked as expected on my 30" Samsung gas convection oven - NX58R4311SS. Be careful when removing the gas pipe to remove and replace the ignitor. I used a magnetic tip screw driver and broke the tip off because the screw was on there SUPER-snug. Use something solid steel. But as stated, the ignitor itself works great. Review 5: rating 5.0. Fits and Works. IT was an exact copy of the part being replaced, it installed and worked without an issue save for it not being easy to reach the location where the unit is plugged into the oven. This is replacing an igniter on an only 3.5 year old Samsung range. I hope this one holds up better than the original. A local appliance repair would have charged in the neighborhood of $200 to replace this part. It took me about 30 minutes. It would have taken an experienced Tech less tha... Review 6: rating 4.0. Igniter for Samsung only lasted 14 months This is the second replacement of my igniter for my Samsung oven. It has a twelve month warranty and went out after fourteen months. I would think the life span would be longer than that but buying two of these is still cheaper than buying one from Samsung.

Easy install, took less than fifteen minutes not counting the time cleaning up the range. Heats much faster than old one. Review 7: rating 5.0. Replacement igniter for Samsung NX58H5600S I realized only my oven was not heating up so after a little research concluded that the igniter was bad. I found this one which stated that it was a replacement igniter for Samsung range model NX58H5600S. I received it in a few days after ordering and was very easy to replace. Watch a video on steps to take I order to get this done. Ladies, don’t wait for your man to do this for you…order the part, watch a video and get ‘er done! You will f... Review 8: rating 5.0. Works perfectly. Pandemic requires you to become a mechanic and follow through to keep your appliances in working condition. I hope this part lasts longer than the original: 4 years. Review 9: rating 5.0. $30 and YouTube saved our stove! Our Samsung gas oven died unexpectedly one day, it just refused to ignite. My husband was ready to go look at new ones, but I took to the internet instead. I found that this was a common issue with our 5 year old range, and found a YouTube video that showed how to replace the igniter. I found this part, ordered it and 3 days later have a working oven again. Cheaper than a house call and definitely cheaper than a new oven! Review 10: rating 2.0. Might fit, but doesn't last Bought this for our oven to get it going again. It worked for about 4 months and then failed; 90 day warranty and we are out another 20-30 bucks to replace it. Very frustrating. I will admit, it was easy to install and at least now I know how to do that. Review 11: rating 5.0. High quality money saver oven bake igniter Used it in Samsung gas oven. Following YouTube videos installed it in 10 minutes and gas oven started baking. Gave 4 star for durability because I don't know how long it will last. It is a very good quality product. Review 12: rating 1.0. Easy install JUNK! Easy to install but only lasted 5 months! Nuff said. Review 13: rating 5.0. I had no issue in getting a replacement and the second was fine, arriving three days later. Installing only took as long as it did for me because two of the eight screws I needed to remove were ... Review 18: rating 5.0. 30 minute install, heats to 350 in 5 minutes again Very easy to install, it took me only 30 minutes. The part fits several other models too (mine is different). Photos show the 6 screws to remove, plus there are two screws to attach the igniter. When tested the oven heated to 350 in 5 minutes, it had been taking upwards of 30 minutes. I will ... Review 19: rating 1.0. Less then 5 months I purchased this item in May and it was already bad by October. I didn’t even get 6 months out of this.
Poor quality. Just stopped working. Review 20: rating 5.0. Saved me a lot of money Replaced the ignited on my Samsung oven for cheap. Found YouTube videos showing me how. It took me about 30 minutes and it wasn't too tough, but it does of course removing parts to get the the igniter. A repairman would have added $120 to the repair and my wife thinks I'm a genius.

Really can't seeing it making up to 26 lbs. of ice in a day, and so far has not made 1 drop of ice (10 cubes) in as little as 6 minutes. Had it going for 2 hours and made only 2 baskets full ... reference_output:
rating 5.0. Still happy after 6 years of constant domestic use. I'm updating my remarks from 6 years ago by saying that I remain happy and very impressed with this machine. I use it frequently, several times a week. I've learned to maximize it by doing small loads often.

I still have a lint problem considering my two cats and use a lot of lint rollers and my vacuum cleaner.

Nevertheless, if one is willing to do the manual components of monitoring the wash, this machine is well worth the expense. Very durable and helpful compared to using the coin laundries. If you live alone and don't have laundry hookups, this is a great answer. Easily lifts in and out of the shower for use.

Review on Purchase 2016:
I'm giving this five stars because it surpassed my expectations in every way. It is more substantial and heavier than I expected, and the motor is up to the task of washing a queen bedspread. Plus, I've been using a coin operated laundry for 5 years, and this is so much better than making that journey, waiting in line, and all the other nuisances of sharing facilities. But to be prepared to enjoy this, first toss out any idea you may have of "automatic washing machines". This is not automatic.
1) The connector to the sink is ridiculous and won't work in most cases. But it's easy. I place my washer in the shower and simply unscrew the sprayer head and attach the hose to fill.
2) similarly, the electric cord is too short. I'm using an extension cord but caution that of course no extension should be allowed to drag on the floor depending on where the outlet is because should the hose become detached one may experience a spray of water all over the floor and bathroom which could cause the shock referred to in the directions.
3) The machine will seem to start and stop - that's o.k. The rotator is simply changing direction.
4) Naturally, you will have to drain and refill for as many rinses as are required.
5) the clothes seem to get thoroughly washed, but cat hair is still a problem. Electric dryers remove cat hair. The filter in this washer does collect lint, but there is still quite a bit of cat hair on the clothes after a round in the spin drier. Never mind. Cat hair is a manual process anyway.
I've been using this machine about a week nearly every day and am pretty happy with it.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Works Great First I recommend not trying what I did.  I tried still using the inverted method because I like to gently shake the immersed coffee to mix.  What happened is hot steam and coffee shot up through the valve and all over me.  So I went to the standard method.  Love that it does not drip.  I have tried metal filters before and don't like trying to clean the tiny particles out of them.  So I wet down a paper filter and stuck that over the metal filter.  I got an excellent cup of coffee (nothing espresso like).  Clean up was a breeze, just like it is with a paper filter and the standard cap.  Just pop it into the garbage.  I did have an issue with it sticking too far down into my coffee mug, so I cut a hole out of a plastic jar lid and using that as a spacer it solved my problem.  Long story short, with a few tweaks I really like this and compared to other similar items, it is a good price.  I also wish to thank whoever packaged this in a sturdy little box!  Now that Amazon is shipping a lot of stuff in the wimpy plastic bags, a lot of items arrive beat up, bent, etc.  The little box protected the item well.  Otherwise it could have easily been ruined in shipping.

reference_output: rating 1.0. Kitchenaide dishwasher.  Stopped leak for a few days, then leaked again Item worked to stop the leak for a few days, then one day we're swimming again.  Before inserting, I greased it with silicone and wrapped white plumbers tape (Teflon) around the outside as others suggested.  After  a few days, the leak came back.  Returned this one and purchased the more expensive Orange gasket $25 and so far no leaks.  I think the mold used to make these just is not exact enough and while all the cheap gaskets on Amazon look similar, I've gone through three so far from various venders and ONLY the orange gasket seems to be holding.  So, spend the extra $15 and get the orange one, your time is worth it.

rating 5.0. Factory part My Kenmore washer developed a tear in this bellow after a mere 12 years. It caused the washer door to leak every time we ran the washer. This part isn’t exactly cheap but it cost a whole lot less than a new washer (and dryer) especially when you consider there was nothing else wrong.

Installation wasn’t super simple, requiring some disassembly of the machine but it really wasn’t as bad as it looked.

And they call both of these "PB040001", and consider them drop-in replacements. NOT TRUE. Be sma...
persona: This customer prioritizes functional reliability and performance in appliance parts, prefers high-quality components that deliver consistent ignition, and values replacements that match or exceed original specifications, while being cautious about design flaws like coil-style igniters that underperform.
reference_output: rating 2.0. Avoid Coil-Style I have replaced the igniters on my Viking range every 18 months since we’ve owned this pathetic appliance starting about 1999. I have learned to avoid coil style igniters but made a mistake on this order. Installed anyway. As expected, one burner did not work.  I can tell from looking at them that they barely get hot enough and glow very orange. Better igniters will glow almost white. I would avoid this style igniter.

reference_output: rating 5.0. A girl can successfully do it! ☺️ I love this filter! It came with all the needed parts to make it happen. The way I did mine was even easier than if I had used the included valve because  it didn’t fit my copper waterline connector so I used one of the included 1/4 adapters to my copper line. reference_output: rating 5.0. Totally Satisfied Customer! My sister bought a countertop icemaker and paid over $300. I wanted one but could not afford that price. With the size and shape, it fit perfectly on my crowded countertop, It was a breeze to set up with the step by step manual provided, I had perfect ice in no time and the taste is exceptional. reference_output:
rating 3.0. Great Icemaker but poor design This is my third machine. Make sure you get an extended warranty. These ice makers need regular cleaning and even with will grow a biofilm on the internals which is near impossible to clean without breakdown. The week point of the machine is the internal water tank and diaphragm pump which will fail over time. Parts are easy to find but replacement is a pain. Two simple remedies fly hat would improve performance.

1. Add a UV sterilization unit on the internal tank and the main tank and well as addition of a submicron filter which is replaceable.

2. Add a removable air filter for the compressor coils.

Vinegar is ok for cleaning but you really need to regularly flush and icemaker solution through the unit. Bleach will corrode the auger and ice making unit so don’t even try.

persona: This customer values functional, reliable products that enhance convenience during outdoor activities, particularly camping, and is willing to make repeat purchases for items that deliver consistent performance and practical benefits.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Nina WOW! Got this for a long term RV trip.  I am amazed by what this unit can do.  It has been able to handle a couple of huge bath towels without a hickup.  I would get cramps trying to wring out a small amount of clothing and when I would hang the hand wrung items on a line they would start dripping after a few minutes.  Not with this spinner!  Just a few minutes and the clothes come out almost dry.  Never any drips on the line after using this.  I like the simplicity of this unit.  Stuff, close lid it starts, open lid it stops, remove almost dry stuff.  I don't even worry about doing any wringing before I put the wet things in the spin dryer.  I have a system when I have to do more than one load of laundry.  Do my first batch, throw it in the dryer, collect some of the water from the dryer to add to my second wash. (hate to waste soap, and water!)  Do my second wash,  dump my wash water, put the dried laundry into my first rinse water while I dry my second batch of washed items.  Then repeat the rinse process.  Just for curiosity I ran laundry in the Nina out of a HE washer and found I could reduce the drying time for my HE dryer!

Review 1: rating 3.0. Very slow and a clumsy design. This item is an interesting concept and although it does work it doesn't work well enough.

Pro's

1. Compact design, this item will fit in the corner of the room but bear in mind its about 5 foot tall when working.

2. Nice and easy to setup, took me all of 5 mins to open box and setup

3. Quiet. The machine is very quiet and you could easily use this in an upstairs apartment without annoying those below you.
If you check local Craigslist I'm sure you'll find others trying to get rid of theirs too, I personally saw 6 listed here in LA.

rating 3.0. It is OK if you have no other option. It takes too long to dry the cloth. One cycle of 180 minutes is not enough, maybe 360 minutes will do the job.
I was leaving the cloth hanging without the bag after the 180 minutes cycle, and 24 hours later it was dry.
The other issue is that it is bulky and warms up the place. I got it for drying delicate close but a regular dryer does a better job.
If there is no way to for you to get a regular dryer, then I recommend it.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Seam Bursted on the filter causing water to leak all night Woke up to a beautiful puddle of water around my fridge this morning. Changed the filter last night checked for leaks and after 30 min I put the tray back in. After using every towel and rag in my house i took out the filter and noticed a crack and the seam. Put in another one to make sure it wasn't the fridge, 24 hours in, and zero leaks. Hopefully, they have better QC for everyone else that buys these. Review 2: rating 1.0. It is incompatbile!!! It is incompatible with Samsung refrigerator that keeps saying we need a new filter. What a ripoff Review 3: rating 1.0. Leaks! Tried two filters. Causes water dispenser to drip Water dispenser drips since installation of this filter. I will have to get a new one as it’s likely to spill onto our floor if left too long without wiping. My water supply lines to the fridge are clean and no hard water build up either. Overall, the price is great especially for a 3 pack. But the quality is poor. Thats why I leave it as a 2 star. By the way I am a mechanic by trade and this was definitely not my 1st time replacing filters, etc. Review 6: rating 5.0. Happy! Worked for my Samsung. Water comes out fast and very clean. Very happy with my purchase. Review 7: rating 3.0. Does not fit These filters are not as long as the Samsung filter from the flange to the O-ring tip. Because of this design flaw it will not twist on easily on some models and will leak if you need to use much more for ce than required for the Samsung filter. I trimmed the flange in order to allow the filter to sit a little deeper. It twisted on easily and worked with no leaks. Review 8: rating 5.0. Works great! We bought this I. The hopes that it would work with our fridge, since the name brand one is $50. It worked wonderfully, so we have now purchased a couple more. Review 9: rating 1.0. Flooded my kitchen. Review 1: rating 4.0. Saved us from having to replace the whole unit This is a nice way to make you clothes washer feel new again. Hope to get another five years of use.

The first time through this was a little hard to get on the washer. I recommend watching a replacement video before you start and rea... Review 2: rating 5.0. Wonderful! Works great! This bellows kit came with everything needed to fix my washer and change out the old bellows kit. It does not come with instructions. It is meant for those that know what they are doing. Do NOT expect this to be an easy job. It took 3 of us to figure it out and we watched a YouTube video to get us through. Next time, I'm calling an expert to do the work. I'll buy the bellows kit here though. Very reasonably priced and excellent shipping. Review 3: rating 5.0. Just what we needed for our Kenmore I don't normally write reviews but I will for this. I installed this on my Kenmore washer, it was the part listed as a replacement part through Sears. It fit perfectly. The instructions were very clear but you needed to read them first then as you were doing it reread each step and it all made sense. A repair man that works on these probably understands the instructions on the first read because they know what parts they are talking about.
A f... Review 4: rating 5.0. Just like the original -- minus the mold and mildew. Easier to install than I expected! Our 5-year old Frigidaire Affinity washer was a breeding ground for mold and mildew, nearly consuming the previous door bellows. This was despite the regular wipe-downs and leaving the door open in between washes. Our clothes didn't really smell funky, but the laundry room sure did. Finally I had had enough. After complaining to Frigidaire, they basically told me I was out of luck, and they o... Review 5: rating 5.0. Helpful Tip This was an easy install once I figured out this helpful trick to put the boot seal retaining spring back on. The how-to video is great except I'm pretty sure they demonstrate how to use the spacers improperly. The spacers are used to hold the spring in place while you stretch the rest of it around the boot. Instead of using the plastic end of the spacer to hold the spring in place, flip the spacer around and use the rubber end to hold the spring in place. The rubber end ... Review 6: rating 4.0. Learn to use the tools. Great product. Awful instructions which makes you spend more time than necessary on this project. Easy to do once you figure out how the "spacers" work.

The item itself is a perfect OEM replacement and works as advertised with no leaks. This bellows is a haven for mold so we're regularly spraying with bleach now. I still think we'll need to routinely replace it every so often.

We were just a step away from the appliance store and buying a new washer, but so glad I tried this. It was a little difficult to get it on, but has COMPLETELY fixed our washer. So great to not put all the little socks and things in mesh baggies and cons... Review 8: rating 1.0. No returns?!! Tried to return this item twice, as I ended up not needing the part. I know it's not the sellers fault in any of this, but there has to be a better way to do this. The spring retainers don't work for dog dodo. Maybe the spring on my washer is abnormally strong, but it took 2 of us to stretch that thi... Review 10: rating 5.0. Extended life of washer about to get unnecesarily thrown out Wife was extremely fed up with the design flaw of the washer which would cause moisture to stay in the bellows and mold or mildew would start to form and stink and even give the clothes a bit of a mildewy smell.

There's a trick to mitigating this of course now that we know after the fact; is that after you're done washing, leave the washer door open so that the water can dry out. Unfortunately if the mold had alr... The old bellows is moldy and needs to be replaced. It is odd that a design failure needs to be compensated for by the owners of their product. These should be a recall item and replaced for free. I have purchased my last product from this manufacturer and won't buy another front load washer again. Poor design and... Review 12: rating 2.0. But installing the new one has three difficult parts (plenty of youtube videos to watch to get a sense... but they gloss over the hard parts):
1. You have to work/knead the be... Review 15: rating 4.0. Straightforward, yet physically challenging install of a functional product. I'm not sure whether to give this five stars or one star. Five stars because it fixed our leaky washer and cost a whole lot less than a new machine or a repair service would have, and because it does exactly what it promises: replaces the door gasket on a Frigidaire washer.
On the other hand, installing the *#%^?! thing is somewhat challenging. You need to work the gasket over a lip on the edge of the d... Review 16: rating 2.0. persona: This customer values functional, space-saving solutions that are easy to install and reliably secure; they prioritize straightforward assembly and long-term performance over minor cost differences, and are willing to pay for a product that eliminates frustration and delivers immediate, hassle-free results.
reference_output: rating 4.0. Good product, difficult installation I would have given this part 5 stars if it wasn't so dang difficult to install. The little spacer things are almost useless, as they don't really stay in place unless someone is holding them, and even then, don't always do what they are supposed to. It took my husband and I working on this together for a while to get the spring on. He was pulling as hard as he could and could barely get it, and he's a pretty strong guy. The bellows itself is fantastic, and I'm glad that I don't have a nasty-smelling mildewed one anymore!

Review 1: rating 5.0. This is a good replacement OEM motor for my 30 + years old ... This is a good replacement OEM motor for my 30 + years old dryer.
The instructions were reasonably clear. Note that this motor will retrofit many models so it may require some minor customizations (instructions include them all in a brief manner).
The metals slotted bands which hold the motor in place are tricky to remove and you could get hurt from their sharp edges. Using a screw driver to stretch the bands ... Review 2: rating 5.0. Dryer runs like new We have a 28 year old KitchenAid dryer that stopped working. The motor would simply buzz when we pushed the start button. When I could get it running it would stop before the drying cycle completed. I ordered this new motor, and with the help of several videos on the internet learned how to install it. It turned out to be easy job requiring just a few basic tools. If you are replacing an old motor you will need to cut off and replace a couple of the connector... Review 3: rating 5.0. DIY saved hundreds Fixed my dryer for less than 70 dollars. Plus found 14 dollars in loose change inside the door frame of the dryer.

Found great tutorials on YouTube stepping me through the project. Review 4: rating 5.0. Good quality motor The motor in my Whirlpool dryer (model LGV7858AW0 purchased in 1993) stopped working. It was taking longer to start the drum turning, especially with heavy loads, and eventually would just hum loudly then shut off. There seemed to be a lot of negative comments on almost any source of a new motor, but I finally decided on this one, although there are cheaper prices elsewhere, because I trust Amazon to stand behind products sold on their site. The new motor looked... Review 5: rating 5.0. Great product, easy to install DIY fits like a glove. Excellent product, direct fit and easy plug and go. There are a few good videos on YouTube about how to replace this yourself, trust me with a little patience you'll be glad you went this route. However I will say be sure to check the other easier fixes such as the fuse for the heater and the actual mode selection dial... Open it after disconnecting the power and chec... Review 6: rating 4.0. Mexican made motor, with delivery direct from Amazon.com, LLC. -- works good, instructions printed poorly I purchased this motor through "Amazon.com LLC". persona: This customer prioritizes functional connectivity and secure fittings in ductwork, but is highly sensitive to material thickness and structural integrity, rejecting products that feel flimsy despite adequate design features.
reference_output:
rating 1.0. Really thin duct - JUNK USED FOR: Connection from clothes dryer to wall exit.
PROS: Both the "male" and "female" ends are ridged and work well for connecting incoming/outgoing pipe.
CONS: Although the accordion like pipe appears strong when fully retracted at 2 feet long; when you expand it you realize the
pipe is just slightly thicker than using your aluminum baking foil (well not quite).

If you intend to expand the collapsed length of 2 feet at all, the pipe will buckle like a politician who's up for reelection.
I also had a generic 10 foot piece of 4 inch expanding aluminum duct that I had purchased previously for this project,
and it was at least two to three times stronger/thicker than this POS.

Review 13: rating 5.0. Perfect fit and operation Filters worked great, exactly as intended. Many thanks to previous reviewers who recommended this as a user friendly alternative to the exorbitantly priced RPWFE that GE tries to force on the consumer. The simple removal of the chip from the bypass filter, taping the chip to the right spot in the refrigerator, and installing the Waterdrop filter gave me a new filter for a fraction of what GE would charge. See previous reviews for the instructions about the b...

Review 14: rating 5.0. Great buy Worked perfect for our icemaker

Review 15: rating 5.0. Great replacement at a fraction of the cost Great replacemnt of the GE OEM. Easy to install. Look at the housing back wall at different angles until "you see" a circular shape in the plastic itself...this is the location of the refrigerator filter sensor...make sure to tape the chip so it's right ov...

Review 16: rating 5.0. Work well I opted to try these instead of spending significantly more money on the GE brand. They fit in the fridge and work well. Our water tastes good and I have not identified any issues with this product. I will definitely purchase again.

Review 17: rating 5.0. Great price, no leaks I use these instead of the stupid E version of these. reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Nice looking, works well, and was not damaged during shipping. Well protected by packaging. This is a good-looking beverage cooler we purchased to put in our RV. It doesn't seem to chill down to the upper 30's as implied in part of the description, but it does work well, and for our purposes on weekender trips it will be entirely adequate as a budget alternative to spending 2 grand to replace the 3-way RV unit in our older RV that probably isnt even worth that much money overall. Review 2: rating 5.0. I love this cooler Update: a few years later and still going strong. This little "chiller" outperforms any I've had in the past. It keeps drinks very cold despite the fact that it has a glass door. It's installed in a bar, and for nicely. Make sure you have a little bit of room for ventilation. I like the soft glow the light provides, but you can turn that off if it's not for you. The racks are somewhat adjustable so I've had everything from a pony keg too a cooler full of 12 oz... Review 3: rating 5.0. Excellent beverage refrigerator. Exactly what I was looking for & keeps drinks very cold. Shelves are removable to accommodate different size bottles. Review 4: rating 5.0. Needs adequate air circulation Works great now! Very satisfied after 5 months.

Initial install was under counter-top but, open on each side about 4in. There was not enough air movement/ventilation and the auto shut-off was tripping because of the high heat build-up.

Be sure to heed the note to allow for adequate air circulation around and on top. Not successful for under counter install. rating 3.0. I just had to give this one three stars I really considered returning this because for the first month or so it seemed to never actually close or seal. I think after a little while, the seal was broken in or something because now it seals pretty well.

This does get your drinks plenty cold, but the controller is very finicky. At 1 number setting your drinks are perfectly chilled, half a number higher and they are freezing and exploding.

The light inside is a neat little feature.

persona: This customer values high-quality, functional filters that effectively prevent coffee sediment, prioritizes trusted brands like Melitta, and is willing to continue purchasing based on consistent performance and unique product availability.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Unbleached Coffee Filters are a plus! We are totally happy with the Melitta brand coffee filters and to be completely truthful we haven't really see the #1 cone filter manufactured or distributed by any one else in our area.  We use them in our one cup coffee pot which has a built in filter, however sometime finely ground coffee can still make it into your cup causing sediment in the bottom of your cup of joe and we've have found that these filters help keep the coffee grounds where they belong..., in the filter brew basket!  We believe we got a swell price on Amazon for 12 boxes and we are now set for practically the whole year.  Hope this helped you decide if there right for you as well.

reviews:

Every now and then you’ll catch it making a whinnying noise but you have to stop what your doing and listen for it but it’s not all the time. None issue for our family. The ice it produces is a bit softer than the ice at restaurants... and honesty I like it even better. Friends and family all like to take a cup home.
So we are a family of 7 and it keeps up with us. I tried to store it in the freezer... Review 3: rating 4.0. gave bad review...want to retract. "Worked great for 6 months, then suddenly stopped pumping water and making ice. One week after the "product support" period ended." This was my previous review. Further investigation reveals there is an easy fix. Apparently the pump sometimes gets a bubble in just the right place and won't move water. (This happened once...after over 6 months of perfect performance). The fix was easily found on YouTube. Turns out that a simple siphon bulb to ... Review 4: rating 1.0. This awful ice maker does not work, worked once, now is a fire hazard cuz it gets do hit. Cant return thru Amazon, WTF. You take my money, so stand behind!! OPAL Will not repair or replace without “video proof”

They told me to contact the manufacturer. The manufacturer told me to contact Amazon. I have called, emailed, with no resolution. I am out $$ for a unit that doesn’t work, and seem to have no recourse. But they have my money! Unbelievable. Review 11: rating 5.0. Maybe you might get one that works, maybe not. But it’s not worth the risk. Amazon is very difficult to... Review 13: rating 5.0. Great ice nuggets Love the ice nuggets. LOUD [[VIDEOID:803f9a77f75673d8f69e21a80b310d16]] This machine is loud. Not kinda loud. LOUD. I called and asked to return this item. They will not take it back due to “hazardous material”. They offered me $100. I said how about $550 back? They sent me another one for free. Its even louder. The ice is amazing because I use Ozarka water to make it. But I am having a hard time remembering the last time I was so disappointed in a product. Review 15: rating 5.0. Pellet Ice!! If you love pellet ice then this is the unit for you. Just be careful putting any pressure on the plastic tub or you will break it. As far as in... Review 6: rating 5.0. Fits perfectly. Fit perfectly. There are clutches out now that have 6 pads instead of just the three. I assume they would last longer.

If your washer is not spinning always check the lid switch first. Let the machine reach the spin cycle by starting at the rinse cycle (don't start in spin). If nothing happens when it gets to spin, try again, this time with the lid up and a pencil or other non-conductive object holding the switch down. If it doesn't spin this time, rele... The solution turned out to be another C-clip, only not the $10 + $7 shipping one online, I went to the local hardware store and...

reference_output: rating 5.0. Great fit. Solved my problem. First, let me say that Amazon does a great job giving you the ability to enter your model number to insure that the part fits. This part was available at a local parts store for considerable more.  As always, Amazon saves the day.  Extremely satisfied.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Good for what it is So far so good! It dries as advertised, perhaps a bit longer than you'd like. reference_output:
rating 4.0. Required mounting modification to fit. Was happy to find this motor priced reasonably. I misinterpreted the "Genuine Replacement Part - Whirlpool item" to be a "Whirlpool" part. It is a FSP "approved" replacement motor from China. I wasn't about to hunt down and wait longer for a "Whirlpool" part so this was installed. The alignment tabs were slightly different for our application because the old motor oriented the wires at about 2 o'clock, the new motor was at 10 o'clock that rubbed the drum assembly. This motor would not fit in that orientation and had to be rotated so the wiring was at the 7 o'clock position. Two of the plastic locator tabs had to be cut for that placement. The ground wire barely had enough slack for this rotational change. Luckily there was just enough but I would have preferred to not put stress on the wire. All said and done, the motor started up fine and is humming along.

Additional thoughts, the push on connector tabs were smaller than the originals so there isn't a lot of tab contact with the connectors. May consider replacing the felt around the drum for a tight seal if your dryer is older. Most the time spent on this repair was vacuuming out the old dust. On this 24 year old dryer it was quite thick, about 25 minutes of vacuuming and wiping dust from inside and backside. 15 minutes to get the motor placed and clipped down. Total time from disassembly to push the button and go was about an 1 hour 15 minutes.

Original motor and replacement motor pics. No picture after cutting the mount tabs so that the connector wires would clear the drum.

reference_output: rating 3.0. Decent quality hose, difficult to install This seems like a decent quality hose and should last well once installed.  Installation however was challenging. It was very difficult to stretch the end around the rigid coupling, and I was quite nervous that I would tear a hole in the hose or not get it properly in place and have serious leaks. Fingers crossed that it'll be OK now, but as soon as practical, I plan to replace this hose with rigid duct.

reference_output: rating 1.0. Junk! Junk!  Even though it lists our model here, the belt was 2” too short, the wheels were squeaky and the pulley would not spin freely. Review 1: rating 5.0. Works well Finally a fair priced alternative to brand name filters... these work and fit as they should Review 2: rating 5.0. Money Saver! Excellent value for the money. I received two filters for just a few dollars more than what the major brand would have cost me. Filter was east to install and works perfectly fine. Review 3: rating 5.0. Compatability excellent price This product is a great value compatible with GE refrigerator and easy to install Review 4: rating 4.0. Refrigerator filter It works as well as the GE filters, however I did have a little difficulty with install. I slowed down and after the install went well Review 5: rating 5.0. !! FINALLY, a replacement for GE's FE style filter that is so expensive! Review 6: rating 5.0. Finally... A less expensive alternative. After being appalled at the price of the GE-branded filters, and failing miserably at "hacking" a cheap filter, I was pleasantly surprised to see these filters pop up. While not cheap-cheap, they're more reasonable and seem to work great so far. Easy to install with no leaks. It runs clear after about a half a gallon flush. Fingers crossed.

[6-month update]: Well, 8 month update. Despite the "Change Filter" warnings from the r... Review 7: rating 1.0. Did not fit! My GE Profile refrigerator specified this filter, but these did not fit. I bought a GE brand filter and it installed easily. Review 8: rating 5.0. Perfect replacement for brand name GE filter. Great replacement for the more expensive brand name filter. Review 9: rating 1.0. Didn't Work. Although installing was as easy as the oem, it leaked badly. Had to return. Review 10: rating 5.0. No noticeable difference Love the price. This filter installed in seconds and seems to be equally effective. There’s no discernible difference and until I find reason to believe otherwise I trust the testing/certification. It reset the “filter change” indicator but I’m not sure it’ll let me know when the next change is due so I used a marker to label the actual filter. All good so far. Review 11: rating 2.0. 1 of 2 did not fit. The other was very tight 1 of 2 did not fit. The other was very tight but finally got it to seat in. Review 12: rating 5.0. Good value These things are always a little tough to get lined up and into the slot. Review 13: rating 4.0. Great value for the money Good value compared to GE product Review 14: rating 2.0. Not quite the same as the real one The filters fit fine, but they are not the same quality. It literally took tens of gallons of water to flush through it before the water was clear. A slight odor is present in the ‘clear’ water now Review 15: rating 4.0. Clear water and ice Easy to install, my suggestion is to wet the O ring to keep it from rolling on you. Review 16: rating 5.0. Works like OE They are a direct replacement for the overpriced GE OE and so far I can’t tell the difference, except for the price, they track days just like OE and function as well, if you want to save some money try them you won’t be disappointed. Review 17: rating 5.0. Works as advertised Used the to replace the OEM filter in our GE refrigerator. Installation was quick and easy, especially after watching a You Tube video on the subject. We've not noticed any difference in operation or effectiveness of these filters. We've had it in place for about two months. No error signals from the refrigerator electronics after installation. Review 18: rating 1.0. BUYER BEWARE - DAMAGED MY REFRIDGERATOR!!! I read the Maxblue reviews and purchased a pair thinking I would save some money. Boy, was that a mistake! Installed the filter in my GE PFE28KELFDS refrigerator and started the procedure to bleed the air out of the line by holding a 4-cup measuring cup under the outlet. Started out okay but 20 seconds in, the water shut off and the fridge displayed an error message stating, "Replace Water Filter. Dispenser Off" with a red triangle.
persona: This customer prioritizes cost-effective, reliable replacements that deliver functional performance and save money compared to original equipment or professional repair services, and values straightforward, DIY-friendly solutions with clear installation guidance.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great Savings over GE Filter So far no complaints with these filters. I can't really do a complete review since it was so recently installed. Will have to see how it fares up to the 6 month life compared to OEM GE RPWFE filter. I am hopeful, and the price is so much fairer than the $50/filter that GE charges.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Cumbersome to install but doable My husband and I installed the kit. It was more troublesome to remove the top and front of the dryer to get to the pilot. The first valve was simple to replace because it was in front. You definitely need a wrench because you can’t get a screwdriver in to unscrew it. The back valve was trickier because it’s in the back and hard to see what you’re doing. The valve went in fine. Just remember to switch the sleeve to LP from N or else you won’t be able to get the screw holes to line up once the new valve is in. Other than that we were able to do it.

persona: This customer prioritizes precise fit and functional performance when replacing appliance parts or filters, values cost savings without compromising quality, and is likely to make repeat purchases based on affordability, reliable performance, and seamless installation.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Parts Fit Perfectly First of all I installed these parts on a Samsung DV405ETPAWR/AA electric dryer.  The dryer was purchased in 2012 and has operated fine with the OEM parts since then.  The other night we heard a loud clunk from the laundry room and, upon investigation, we found that the dryer had stopped spinning even though there was still time on the timer.  I removed the top and the belt was loose on the drum.  I immediately thought that the belt had broken.  I looked online and Lowe's had a universal replacement belt, so I took a trip to Lowe's and purchased one.  I watched several videos on You Tube on belt replacement, got out my tools, turned off the power, and started to tear the dryer down.  It turns out that the belt was not broken but the tensioner had self destructed, the belt had slipped off what remained, and the "broken belt switch" had tripped.  I started looking at Amazon and found that this kit was available.  I had also checked out the rollers and two of them were pretty stiff, so I decided to replace all 4 of them while I had the dryer apart, and keep the two good ones for spares.  The shipping was very prompt and I received this kit via UPS 2 days later.  Since I don't have much room in our laundry, the worst part of the job was removing the rear rollers since it required access to the back of the dryer to remove the 7 screws that secure the plate holding the rollers.  I did try to get the rear rollers off without removing the back panel using a 17mm and 8 mm wrench, but the nuts were on so tight I could not get them loose.  After taking the rear panel off, I did use a impact driver to loosen the nuts and remove the rollers.  If you have a lot of room to move around the dryer to gain access to these screws, it should not be an issue.  I quickly re-assembled the dryer tested it, and It worked perfectly.  Since I only completed this repair yesterday, and my wife only ran one load of clothes, I don't have an opinion on the longevity of the parts.  I will update this review after using the dryer for a few months.  The price was really great and I hope they last.

persona: This customer prioritizes hygiene and convenience, especially during health concerns, and values compact, easy-to-install appliances that function effectively in small living spaces without permanent plumbing.
reference_output: rating 5.0. MUST GET Because of the "Stay at Home" order, I was afraid to go out to the public laundry because of people spreading the Covid19 germs. I bought this dryer and I bought the matching washer.  I live in an apartment with no hookups, so the washer is hooked up at the bathroom sink.  Super easy.  I set the dryer up by a small window, bought an aluminum dryer vent hose, used a heavy piece of cardboard, cut it to fit the window opening (just enough open for the vent hose), cut a hole in the cardboard, pushed the end of the vent hose into it, set the cardboard fitting into the window, pulled window down securely, used good aluminum vent tape (not duct tape) around the vent hose to secure it to cardboard, and around window opening to make an air tight fitting.  Also covered the cardboard with something pretty I cut out, not really necessary. I left the screen in the window so no bugs or little critters can  in.  I also bought an aluminum elbow vent to use at the connection to the dryer to turn the vent hose in the upright position to aim towards the window.  This adds a few inches, and pulls it away from the wall.  I also bought a little 12x18 furniture dolly at Harbor Freight for $8, just to raise it off the ground to make it easier for me to reach. You could put this dryer on a small table or desk, but this works for me.  The important thing is to make sure you push the little button in by the dial -- that is what turns the heat on.  Now I do not have to go to a laundry mat or to a friend's house except when I want to wash big heavy blankets.  My clothes are clean, and dry, and smell good.  The lint screen is a little weird to get used to -- just reach in there and give it a little twist and pull straight out.  It comes apart and you peel the lint off like normal.  The felt cover is to prevent extra lint going thru the vent, it goes back into the back first, so just put your two pieces back together, then place the felt on the back, then push back into the dryer.  That's why I lifted my dryer, so I could see in there a little better.  You will wonder why you waited so long to get one of these.  The washer and dryer have a two year warranty if you go back to the HomeLabs website and register them.  If you live in a neighborhood where you don't feel comfortable with the vent in the window, you can get those little screw in locks to lock the window down and it won't move.  Or you could buy an indoor vent box.  I have asthma, and don't like to blow anything with dust or lint into my living area, and the window vent works for me.  Also, when I am drying clothes,  tend to run my bathroom fan which draws air out of the apartment and takes it outside.  It just keeps things circulating inside so there is no buildup of humidity.  (I live in a state where there is a lot of humidity already.). This little washer and dryer can go with me anywhere I live in the future.  They are sturdy and well built.  Just a miniature version on the the normal size washers & dryers.  You will love them!

reference_output:
rating 4.0. Recycle Hack Well, anyone who owns a Samsung French Door Refrig with a POS Ice Room Ice Maker knows that even after applying all the fixes, they'll still be hair-dryer defrosting it, every ~3 months; I've got it down to ~15 minutes now. So instead of buying a new refrigerator-with-ice-maker-in-the-freezer-drawer (at C0stc0), I decided to limp it along another year and buy another filter. There's just the two of us in the household and I've calculated we dispense <300gal through the filter annually PLUS we have a clean water supply, so I ignore the 6 month reminder and replace it every December.
The filter is a simple plastic 1/4" thick, two piece plastic cartridge with 3 rubber o-rings, housing the carbon-filter insert. It's not difficult to disassemble the filter for recycling/disposal:
1) remove the o-rings with an awl or precision mini bladed screwdriver. shake out the water.
2) clamp the filter in a vise or brace appropriately
3) (hack)saw 1/4" deep around the diameter at the cartridge seam, about 1/3 up from the bottom.
4) discard the carbon-filter insert and recycle the two plastic cartridge pieces.
Maybe call your recycler and confirm they recycle Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS) (7).
Remember to run 3 gallons of water through your new filter. Maybe water the plants, fill a toilet tank, or clean the sinks and use it for rinse water.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Easy to install, will save me ~$80/yr I installed this in my GE Profile fridge because I have a whole-house water filter and the WMF filters now cost over $40. This OEM bypass arrived with no instructions, so I followed a YT video's advice: shut off water intake valve; rotate the installed WMF filter counter-clockwise and then pull down to remove; insert the bypass in the filter's place and turn clockwise about 1/2 turn; turn on the water intake valve; dispense some water to get out any air bubbles and check for operation. It worked fine.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. These Work Great, and Here's a Helpful Hint I bought these for use with my Keurig K-40 Coffee Maker. They work great, and are a great value. Highly recommended.

After cleaning my Keurig recently and inserting a new water filter, I was chagrined to discover that the coffee maker wasn't working properly -- it didn't seem to be priming properly and would whir and whir endlessly, with no output.

After a bit of troubleshooting, I realized that air was trapped in the water filter assembly, and was apparently causing the pump not to prime properly. So here's a tip: Follow the directions for preparing a new water filter (soak at least five minutes in tap water and rinse to remove any loose charcoal particles). Then when inserting into the water reservoir, gently "tilt" the filter assembly to near-horizontal. This will allow any air trapped in the filter assembly to escape (evidenced by bubbles). Finally, re-affix the filter to the intake on the bottom of the water reservoir and you should be good to go.

rating 1.0. Loud & only lasted a few days! Keep shopping for a better replacement pump! Replaced the 15 year old original because it was getting very loud (buzz/hum) when draining. This replacement was MUCH LOUDER. I was immediately concerned, but it seemed to work ok…Three days later it quit working completely and I had to reinstall the original! I just ordered the Askoll brand that is a bit more expensive, but looks to have much better reviews. I’ll update this post with those results.

*update* the Askoll has been in place for over a month now and is doing great. Much quieter and works as it should. Unfortunately, I forgot to get the other one returned and it’s too late now - I’m going outside now to shoot it… pow pow.
*1 Year Update* the Askoll is still working great.
