Review 1: rating 5.0. Greatest water filters for Keurig I have EVER bought!! These individually wrapped charcoal filters for Keurig are expertly made, and if you follow the instructions on the bag  correctly, then you'll have ZERO issues of tasting problems with your tap water. I live in a large city where the put chlorine, fluoride and god knows what else. These filters, HONESTLY, make my cup of coffee taste so clean and ONLY like coffee. Before these K&J water filters, my coffees would always have a une...

Review 2: rating 5.0. Worked great and coffee delicious I ordered my Keurig filters from K&J after seeing the difference in price between the two. Six dollars for 6 versus $36 encouraged me to try them. Received package in 2 days my filter in the Keurig looked identical to the new ones. Soaked for 5 minutes, snapped it right in and then ran approximately 4-5 cups of plain hot water. Worked great and coffee delicious.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Finally, the correct size! I had given up trying to buy these a couple of years ago. Every time I ordered, regardless of the dimensions displayed, they were too small.  I moved toward using already filtered water in the bin.  Something caused me to start looking again, and as I read the description (regarding the larger and smaller filters) and related comments, I figured I'd give it one more shot.  Glad I did!  They're the large(r) ones!

Review 4: rating 5.0. Take care of your Keurig, folks! Keurig coffee machines are interesting inventions.  I honestly hate the idea of them as they generate tons of unnecessary waste with the awful disposable k-cups.  I use a refillable k-cup with freshly ground coffee, so no horrible amounts of waste from my machine.  Also, the machine itself can get quite stinky and musty (think fish tank) if you don't actually take them apart and clean them every so often.  These filters also help hard water deposits f...

They are not authentic "Kuerig" filters. Asking for refund.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Simply remove the three screws along the front bottom of the washer and remove the bottom panel. After removing the panel, slowly unscrew the large plug (drain). Here is an excellent trick: bring over your wet/dry vac. As you slowly open the drain plug, hold your vacuum tube right up to it. I bought the paper filter to try to save time with the tap tap tap and rin... Review 4: rating 2.0. In either case, this does not allow you to put in the whole amount of grinds, thus resulting in a weaker cup of coffee.. If you like it weaker, then not a bad choice.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. This is a wonderful washer! I bought this washer back in 2011 when I lived in apartment with no washer dryer hook up and really couldn't afford to to use the laundry room in the complex every week ($9.00 total for two washes and one dryer!). I have used this washer since 2011. It has gotten me through re-washing clothes after finding black mold in an old building we use to live in, washing cashmere sweaters, and even washing cloth diapers (there is a trick to doing that, but it doesn't take too long once you figure it out).

Although we now have a bigger washer, I still keep this one for small loads, cashmere, and in case something happens (power goes out and diapers need washing). I recommend this washer for everyone (campers, people living off grid, people who live in small apartments, everyone!).

reference_output:
rating 4.0. A tea drinkers friend Difficult to use without a filter - the pressure of the incoming water ends up making a hole in the middle of the coffee grounds so you don't get a very strong brew. I have taken a standard ribbed, coffee filter and put it inside the cup, cutting off the excess. This helped and made it a lot easier to clean after use. My hubby still complained that the coffee was weak.

I am using with loose leaf tea with great success. Paper filter, cut to fit and filled 2/3'ds full with tea. Two brew cycles, 1 on 3-4 cups and 1 on 4-5 yields two-quarts of brewed tea with enough space for plenty of sweetener.

If you want for brewing carafes of coffee, I would say keep looking but for tea, it works very, very well.

persona: This customer prioritizes product performance and application effectiveness, particularly in coverage and adhesion, and is likely to avoid repeat purchases if the product fails to deliver consistent results across larger surfaces despite being willing to use multiple coats on small areas.
reference_output:
rating 2.0. not enough goop The caps are great but the goop itself was the problem.
- 1/2 ounce is not a lot and it dries too fast.
-it doesn't cling to metal well especially hard if covering a big area (one inch or more)

It does work fairly well on small spots where you can put more than one coat. Putting more than one coat on a larger stretch is nearly impossible because you need the first coat to stick good before you can get the second to flow on.

This product is perfect if your rack has only minor problems like wear on the tips of the tines. Using the goop as glue for the caps works great, other applications fall short in my opinion. My basket had more damage than this little 1/2 ounce bottle could fix unfortunately. There are other similar products here on Amazon that come in larger volumes. I was tempted to buy those from the start, but they didn't have as many good reviews as this product so I took a chance that this stuff was so good that a little goes a long way...it didn't.

persona: This customer prioritizes reliable, long-lasting performance and timely delivery, and is willing to invest in high-quality replacements for hard-to-find or essential components, especially when they come with strong customer support and consistent functionality.
reference_output:
rating 1.0. Updated, yet again... 10/17/19 UPDATE 10/17/19: After writing my original review, MaxBlue contacted me, apologized and sent me 4 replacement cartridges after I had returned the original leaky one to Amazon. I was happy, grateful even. This was in May of this year, 2019. They are supposed to last 6 months or through 350 gallons of water. I replaced the filter 3 times since then. I had to as the water coming through and the ice maker began to be slow. Being it's October now and we have NEVER gone through 350 gallons of water through the filter, seems a bit unusual. There are only 2 adults in the house, no kids, no live ins.

Walked into the kitchen Tuesday morning to see half the kitchen floor flooded with water. YIKES! I actually thought a hot water heater line had busted and ran to the basement to shut off the water. The basement was HALF FLOODED with water! When I finally calmed down? I opened the refrigerator door and saw water at the bottom. IT WAS A BROKEN MAXBLUE FILTER, YET AGAIN!!!

See the 4th picture above for the latest atrocity. It took half the morning to clean the kitchen, hallway, and side rooms up just because of a defective water filter in the refrigerator. Thank God we have a sump pump in the basement.

If you need more pictures of the latest, just ask, I still have the broken filter.

MaxBlue is trying to be a good company as they originally replaced my first filter with more than I had ordered, but they lack very much in quality control of their product. Their pricing is good to look at and compare. But after this flood in an older home, I can't afford their products anymore.

If you buy this product? Just be sure to replace it at least every two months. Don't wait for the 3rd!!!

This was so easy to replace on the Samsung refrigerator. Just twist a knob and it was out and replaced. We had an LG refrigerator and that thing was a PIA to replace. This was too easy, lol. The water tastes fine after replacing it and the ice cube make is working fine, with the same great taste. I highly recommend these filters and will be buying them again.

Have to change this to a one star review. I was in the kitchen this afternoon and heard the fridge make a strange sound. Opened the door and water came POURING out! I mean POURING! I shut.off the water line to the fridge, cleaned up the water both inside and out, and saw it was dripping from the filter housing. Took this filter out and saw the cracks in it. Enlarge the pics above and you can see the many cracks in it. I won't be buying Maxblue again.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Chemex Unbleached Square Coffee Filter Excellent Product at a reasonable price (Cost at time of this review was $7.99 per box of 100 filters). The unbleached Chemex coffee filters provide a more pure coffee flavour without adding a chemical taste to the brew that using bleached filters brings. I only wish Chemex sold the unbleached filters in the 12" round unfolded style. Review 2: rating 5.0. Coffee filters These filters are for a Chemex coffee maker. I cannot buy them at my local grocery store, so I order them. They do just exactly what they are supposed to do. The weight of the paper is just right to hold the coffee and water and still let the water drain through the grounds. Review 3: rating 5.0. Still happy after a year! I love my Chemex coffee maker...no more bitter coffee. The filters is what makes the coffee maker so fantastic. As the title states, I've been using them for a year and I'm still happy! Review 4: rating 5.0. reference_output:
rating 5.0. Photos to Illustrate Fix Described by Earlier Rating (Book Lover) I love it when engineers help us out by analyzing a product and improving it (thanks for your rating, Book Lover)! I took a few photos to help explain the problem that Book Lover was helping the rest of us to fix. Three of the attached photos were taken from my old icemaker control board after it was removed, so you can see where the failure happened (and will happen again with your new board if you do not fix it before you install it). The two silver pins on the icemaker (pictured) are part of a heating element that is made of aluminum.

First, you need to get a knife and/or sandpaper and clean the corrosion off of these silver (aluminum) pins. That corrosion is what caused the failure. You can see the photo where the silver pin contacted the old copper circuit panel; the copper to aluminum contact created corrosion and arcing that completely ate away the copper. When you buy this part, also buy some OxGard OX-100 in a tube, to glob over the pins and the contacts on the new board before you put it back together. This seals out oxygen and moisture so it won't corrode. Don't glob too much, because I think it conducts electricity. The pictured tube is 25 years old, left over from when I was putting together sprinkler wires in my yard, but you can buy a new tube on Amazon Prime and have it delivered for less than $4.

The last photo shows where I globbed the OxGard on the copper board where it will contact the pins. I also coated the pins with OxGard. If you are trying to skip steps (like I always do, and break something else), you probably haven't taken your icemaker out of the refrigerator. And the pins are all frosted over and wet. Get a hair dryer and dry them off, so you are not trapping moisture inside the OxGard. Re assemble. It works! Now, it will last longer than the original icemaker. Rise up and defeat planned obsolescence! Defeat THE MAN!

P.S. If you are really cheap, you might try just buying the OxGard (not the new part), clean things up, and make a good connection where it was arcing before. Also take the big gear off and clean the carbon on the back of the gear. But the resistance may have fried some other part of the system, so you decide how much your time is worth.

reference_output:
rating 4.0. Mostly compatible with p/n 8565815... I used this as a replacement for Whirlpool part no. 8565815, which appears to be identical in every way except for the $20.00 price premium and different electrical connectors on the solenoids. (The solenoid, BTW, is the electrical component that flips the dispenser latch open at the appropriate time in the cycle, and also pumps rinse agent in at the appropriate time.)

In my case, I opted to swap my old solenoid in to the place of the solenoid that came with this unit. It's a simple one-screw swap, and I sealed the new solenoid up in a plastic bagge, and taped it to the inside of the dishwasher door panel so it'll be there if the old one ever fails.

Alternatively, I could have left the new solenoid in place, and crimped two common flat blade electrical connectors to the dishwasher wiring.

In any event, this dispenser has the original unmodified, flimsy latch design at the unit it replaces. The dispenser latch becomes difficult, if not impossible, to close over time, hence the reason for replacement.

Replacement is simple enough. My dishwasher required a T15 torx to remove the 8 door panel screws, and a 3/16 socket / driver to remove the 6 mounting screws for the dispenser unit. It's important during reinstallation of the new unit to not overtighten the 6 dispenser mounting screws. I suggest running a cycle or two with the door panel off and observing your work to ensure there's no water leaking past the (included, pre-installed) rubber gasket that seals this dispenser off from the interior of the door panel.

Knocked off a star because of the poorly designed latch mechanism that hasn't been redesigned. I should knock off another star for the price shenanigans, but that is what it is.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Works great Two weeks back I noticed my drier not working as its supposed to, and after a drying cycle all clothes were still damp. A quick test revealed that there is no hot hair coming from the vent

Quick test to verify if the issue is related to the heating element

1) Set the heat setting to Manual Time dry
2) Open the door, press the push button behind the door, and Start the dryer.
3) Wait for a minute or two and check if warm air is blowing out through the vents.

In my case, I had no warm air coming out, I checked on youtube, and found several videos

There is one video, where the guy removes the drum to access the heating element --> You don't have to do it
There is one video, where the guy removes the back panel to remove the heating element --> You don't have to do it

I followed this video, and took almost an hour to replace the element
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_jp7Sq4OMgQ

1) Remove the top cover, there are 2 screws behind the drier, unscrew it, and slide the top panel to remove it
2) Remove the front control panel, probably 4 screws on top.
3) Remove the door. Four screws on top, and 2 screws in the front
4) Remove the support , pull out the heating element housing
5) remove the sensors, and open the housing
6) replace the heating element
7) put it back
8) pretty much follow the video.

If you are unsure, take pictures before you unscrew or disconnect any electrical wires, so you know how to put it back.

reference_output:
rating 4.0. Good ice maker if you need a lot of clear ice and have a place to put it.... My wife was getting hip surgery and was told that she needed to use ice on her hip for at least 3 weeks following her operation. My fridge icemaker was just not going to be able to keep up with the demand, so I started reviewing ice makers. This Fridgidaire icemaker was reviewed well on Amazon, and advertised that it made clear ice. Most importantly, this unit advertised that it could make 40 lbs of clear ice in a day, which is much more than many of the cheaper ice makers. Since I figured it would be nice to use this to make ice in a home bar after she was done with it, I bought this unit.

This unit works by dribbling water over a vertical metal grid that looks like an ice cube tray. The metal is cooled and the water freezes as it pours over the grid. The longer you let the water flow, the more completely the ice cube forms within the grid. You can adjust the thickness of the ice within the grid by a setting on the touchscreen. I set mine to maximum to get solid, clear cubes. When the freezing is done, the water stops flowing and the ice cubes are pushed as a "block of cubes" into a bin.

The Good:
- If you use filtered water, the ice is indeed clear and doesn't have those annoying hollow "holes" in the ice that I associate with cheap ice makers
- The product looks great. I really like the red and black coloring, and it looks good, not cheap.
- It will make 40 lbs of ice in a day if you are diligent about emptying the ice cube bin frequently.
- The ice maker makes about the same amount of noise as a dorm fridge. There is certainly a fairly loud clunk each time a sheet of ice falls into the tray, but the overall ice-,making process was less loud than I expected.

The Bad:
- The ice cube bin holds maybe 4 or 5 "sheets" of ice before it fills up and the machine stops. Since the bin is not cooled, the ice starts to immediately melt unless it is taken out and placed in a freezer or cooler.
- That means about every hour or so, you must go into the machine and remove the ice and put it somewhere cold if you want to max out on the ice production. It would have been much nicer if the holding bin kept the ice cold.

The ugly
- If you fill the unit with water and let it run overnight, you will wake up to 4 or 5 sheets of mostly melted ice in the tray.
- The ice machine works well, but is a bit difficult to drain completely during cleaning. I worry that you could easily leave some water behind which might lead to mold.

Overall Grade: I give this a B+ overall, I used the heck out of this thing for almost two months. I bought some ice machine descaler and cleaner, and cleaned it once during my ice-making marathon. Cleaning and descaling was a breeze. I would have given this a perfect A+ if the ice holding tray area could keep the ice from melting.

rating 1.0. Do not buy this pitcher. Update update:
I emailed the company about the flaky weld seams and there response was their “quality engineer” said its normal! Metal flaking into your milk is supposed to happen?!? Don't give this company your money. They are charging a premium price for a low quality product.

Tl;dr:
If you buy this product, expect thin-flaky weld seams filled with machining residue.

Update:
My replacement was just as bad. Espro manufacturing has serious defects and zero QA. Do not buy this. A real shame since the design seems novel. The pictures are two different pitchers.

Big fat weld seam that will be a breeding ground for bacteria. Ive requested a replacement and will update this if the new one is not defective.

reference_output:
rating 4.0. Great freezer - bad manual... Good freezer, though I think the instructions need to be better... It seems that when the freezer is set to MAX, there is some kind of quick-freeze setting that is not mentioned in the manual. With my freezer, I set the temperature to MAX on the initial use, because it is hot out here in the desert, even in the middle of winter. The freezer refused to go below 10 degrees with about 4 gallons of water inside. In fact, it would go down to 10, then drift back up to 20, before going back to 10.

I moved the freezer, thinking it was not getting enough airflow, and it did the same thing, after testing for two days, I shut it off. The next day, I turned it back on, but only set it to medium. With a few hours, it was at 0 degrees, and by the next day it was below zero.

I think the MAX setting must runt the compressor constantly, then cycle the compressor off after a while to avoid overheating it, while the lower settings can turn the compressor on and off at will.

Again, this was not mentioned in the manual, but another brand of freezer I have has a similar setting, so I should have known. (Should be stated in the manual though.)

I did let the freezer stand for over 24 hours before using it to avoid problems from shipping, though the manual only recommends 2 hours. That seems insufficient, and I would recommend at least 24. Also, the manual states that "too much food" can cause the freezer to run inefficiently. Yes, if packed too tight to allow airflow, i think this is true, but the fuller a freezer is, the better it operates in my understanding. Later it warns against adding too much warm food at a time, which is a separate case.

In any case, the manual could use some serious improvement, but the freeze itself works great..

With out the lid filter piece i still have to pull out the digh soap and sponge and scrub the reusable k-cup lid. The filter with lid combo, I just dump the whole thing out and give it a good rinse and carry on. I full on wash it twice a week.
I knew these we... Review 10: rating 5.0. Amazing These are great, make cleaning the reusable pods way easier. Review 11: rating 5.0. They work Great! saves time when emptying k-cups They work Great! saves time when emptying k-cups. Will definitely order again when I run out. Recommend these for anyone considering reusable filters. Review 12: rating 4.0. Great little filters I love these filters because they take the "sludge" out of the coffee so you can enjoy the whole cup. Nevertheless, I purchased one of those coffeemakers and kept some of the individual cups on hand for when visitors want a single cup of coffee that is not as strong as I like it, or that is decaf (which I just don't drink).

It was a bit of a pain trying to trim this tiny thing to length, but once I did, it works just fine.

Overall diameter = 2-13/16 inches (71 mm). Fits 1/4" (7mm) diameter "D" shaped shaft . reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Efficient-and easy to use! Wow! This machine works great! My knees aren't what they used to be, so going up and down stairs to do laundry has gotten more difficult.
I fit this little washer into my linen closet in the bathroom. It easily pulls out for use next to the bath tub. I purchased a shower head that has a hose on it. This makes filling the machine faster than using the intake hose. The settings are versatile and our clothes get CLEAN! I can't say enough good things abou... Review 2: rating 3.0. Great Product-Spinner quits after 2 months-UGH Love this washer and the spin dryer is fantastic!
I just am up dating this because my spinner just stopped working. I love this machine but now I can not use the spinner. It makes noise but does not spin. I just used it a few minutes before and the second handful of clothes it would not budge. BUMMER Review 3: rating 5.0. Five Stars I love this machine, it cleans my cloth very well and make it bright like new. Review 4: rating 5.0. Giantex - Practical Innovations for people who are not afraid to do a little extra work This was a life saver for me. After our washer stopped working and then our dryer, again, I was just so done. I decided I needed a backup as going to the laundromat is a huge problem for me time wise. My schedule is just way too hectic for that. So after trying hand washing alone and messing up my hands in the process (hello joint pain), I needed another option. This little gem did just that... Review 5: rating 5.0. Love it This washer is so easy to use. I had about 3 1/2 loads of laundry washed (tall hamper full) and it was quiet, cleaned the clothes so well, and rung them out so well that it took no time for them to dry. The directions that come with this are useless, watch one of the videos instead. Best purchase ever. Review 6: rating 5.0. I was so amazed. It came in a box that my landlord ... This machine is awsome, I was so amazed.
It came in a box that my landlord would never know what it was, I'm so happy with my washer and would recommend it to anyone.. Review 7: rating 1.0. Won't last 2 months. DON'T Buy The product only did about 15 loads before the wash motor went out and would no longer agitate. There is no warranty and difficult to contact the seller. I would recommend you do not by this. You will spend more on this dead paperweight than just going to the laundry mat. Don't buy it, it is made with cheap parts and motors. Review 8: rating 4.0. COLLEGE STUDENTS ON A BUDGET YAS I'm a college student who lives on my own in my own apartment and I was just spending way too much money going to the laundromat that I thought, why not buy a unit for my own apartment? And so I did! This thing here is a life saver. It really does wash your clothes extremely well and the spin cycle leaves your clothes like 95% dry and only 5% damp. It's an awesome unit, but it is medium in size and so it only fits like 5 shirts and 3 pairs of leggings... Review 9: rating 5.0. Spinner does a great job. I use mine for shop towels This washing machine works very very well. I am actually impressed. It washes vigorously. Can't tell much of a difference between Normal and Soft setting. I have read reviews on here about people not using the water on the spinner side but what I do is wash a load, drain, then put half of load in spinner, turn water on in spinner and spin for 4 minuntes total....with water for 2 minutes, then turn water off and spin only for 2 ... Review 10: rating 4.0. So far so good, I use a bucket to fill up with ... So far so good, I use a bucket to fill up with the machine I wish the hoses were just a little bigger. Review 11: rating 1.0. Spinner broke Spinner broke after 11 uses and I only spin a little at a time. Very disappointed in this product. Review 12: rating 4.0. Impressed Works better than expected for a small washer, and man, does it get the dirt out of my toddlers clothes. I was shocked to see how dirty the water was! Could use longer and more durable water hose and drain hose though. Overall, I'm impressed. Review 13: rating 4.0. NEED A PUMP I LOVE IT!!! But next time I will purchase one with a pump. Review 14: rating 5.0. Great product. Great product. You need the buy another turkey Baxter in order to adopt your faucet. Review 15: rating 4.0. It works Ok, so it works. But you have to be realistic. You can't expect it to do full size regular loads. It's just not going to happen. But it does a great job for what it is. My average load is a pair of jeans, 3 dress shirts and maybe 2 or 3 T-shirts. My only complaint is that the hoses they come with are garbage. I end up just sticking my shower head into the tub to fill it. It's much quicker that way too.

Pros:
-will do quick loads between trips to the laundroma... Review 16: rating 5.0. Great product Product arrived on time. Package was a little damaged but the washer wasn't. Unfortunately the instructions it comes with are vague and it seems like it was not translated appropriately but I looked at one of the reviews posted on here and her review was of great help. I have done 20 loads so far with no issues. I just recommend that if you do purchase this, keep in mind that it is small and made of plastic so don't over load and don't wash thick items such as comforter... Review 17: rating 3.0. Three Stars I RETURN THIS PRODUCT. WASHER IS OK BUT DRYER IS NOTHING, EVEN SMALL BABY SUMMER CLOTHES NOT SOCK. Review 18: rating 5.0. So far so good I personally only wanted this wash for the spinner however it's been quite a treat to be able to wash too. Most spinners are $140 - $160. So this is great to have an additional component (washer) alongside the spinner. Review 19: rating 2.0. Laundry solution This is a portable washing machine. The brand is Costway. It works by washing clothes in one side and the other side is a spin dryer. The spin dryer leaves the clothes damp. They can then be hung on a clothesline or placed on a drying rack. It does not have a pump so you will need to manually raise the machine higher than the level of the drain to drain the water. It is heavy when full. The washing machine was slightly damaged when I received it (cracking arou... Review 20: rating 5.0. Best Amazon purchase ever Best Amazon purchase ever! Perfect for small loads of laundry. Super easy to use and way faster than a regular washing machine.
persona: This customer prioritizes convenience, hygiene, and efficiency in their purchases, especially for essential household tasks, and values products that solve specific lifestyle challenges with reliable performance and minimal hassle.
reference_output:
rating 5.0. best purchase and customer service ever!!! I'm prego, live on the third floor of a very large apartment complex shared with a lot of other not so clean tenants. the provided laundry room is never clean and very unsanitary. The laundry mat is a hassle and just not going to work for me while prego in the summer. so I stumbled upon this and ordered it. super light, washes a lot more clothing than I thought, quickly, my general thoughts is my whites seem to come out cleaner and brighter, and saves me on detergent, bleach, and softener.

So after a few washes I noticed it had a diesel, lighter fluid type smell (may have just been me due to hytened prego smells) but I was concerned and contacted the seller. I kid u not, they responded to me by the next day, told me to not use it, asked if I wanted a full refund or a replacement without having to send this one back. loved the product so I wanted a new one and I legit had a replacement one in less than 1.5 weeks of contacting the seller.

All in all I watched the provided videos on amazon and it was common sense. this things is so easy to use, and once you've gotten your own hang of it, you'll figure out ways and tricks to it!!

Only thing I do recommend is get a different hose, I got the one that amazon recommends and it works perfectly.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great price This filter fits our whirlpool refrigerator and it was such a great price. I will definitly buy this brand again! Review 2: rating 5.0. Nice economical Water Filter Great price and so far works well on our Whirlpool refrigerator. I had purchased more expensive units at Sears and this seems to work as well at 1/2 the price. Review 3: rating 5.0. AS ADVERTISED !! Usually went to brick and mortar store to purchase our replacement filter for our Kitchenaid frig. I convinced my husband this one was an exact replacement and would fit. Well, it arrived quickly and it was exactly as advertised .... an exact replacement -- it is on the job filtering our water !! Review 4: rating 5.0. Perfect fit, went straight from box and popped right ... Perfect fit, went straight from box and popped right in without any problems. I wish all products were this easy. Would definitely buy again. Review 5: rating 5.0. What I liked about this filter is that water came out tasting ... What I liked about this filter is that water came out tasting clear and clean immediately. In the past, we have purchased filters from other companies that produced several gallons of plastic-tasting water. So, it was a nice change to have a product that seemed to work instantly. Review 6: rating 5.0. good price and fit. It fit perfectly. The price was 1/4 of the original that got discontinued.
The taste? Not so great. I was hoping to have a more clean tasting water. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great product and excellent price. Have only had a month but for this price so impressed. Whirlpool filter cost more than three times as much so even if this only last 3/4 months I will be happy. Better to buy this 3/4 times a year than whirlpool 2x. Highly recommend giving this a try. Review 8: rating 4.0. Looks and installs like every other ufk8001 filter I've bought for the last ... Looks and installs like every other ufk8001 filter I've bought for the last 8 years but it did drip for awhile after I installed it although it seems to be fine now. If it lasts more than 2 months which is my current record I'll be impressed. Review 9: rating 1.0. Fits but leaks bad!! Fits but you can't use as it leaks bad! Review 10: rating 1.0. It is better to have the one that adheres to both 42 ... My water tastes awful with this filter. It only removes half the contaminants of my previous filter. Also, the water ran blue for pitcher after pitcher when I first installed the filter. I never had that happen before with the more expensive filter. I think this is a case of you get what you pay for. This filter only adheres to NSF/ANSI 42 standards. It is better to have the one that adheres to both 42 and 53 NSF/ANSI sta... Review 11: rating 5.0. Water tastes good. I saw a review that mentioned the black ... Water tastes good. I saw a review that mentioned the black debris. I filled one 3 oz water cup and it was a little gray, but second cup was clear and it has been ever since! That 's pretty normal for the beginning of any filter, I think. I especially love the savings!! Review 12: rating 1.0. One Star This filter is not Orem quality Review 13: rating 2.0. Two Stars Fits properly but water flow is much noisier than before Review 14: rating 1.0. Am disappointed. The flow from the water dispenser does not ... Looking for an affordable replacement for Maytag UKF8001, and decided to try the Waterdrop Plus based on the reviews. Am disappointed. The flow from the water dispenser does not flow strong for a new water filter. It's like my old Maytag UKF 8001 after it has been used for 3 to 4 months. Not sure I will buy the Waterdrop Plus again. Have hoped more from the Waterdrop Plus. Review 15: rating 4.0. fit our refrigerator just fine, and the taste of the water is as ... Filter seems well made, fit our refrigerator just fine, and the taste of the water is as good as the factory original filter provided. I wish there was an independent lab that tested these filters, but at least based on what I can see and taste, this seems like a good quality product. Review 16: rating 2.0. Short-lived Premium/Plus model only lasted 2 weeks. Maybe not suitable for well water. Review 17: rating 1.0. Not worth lithe discounted price. Horrible filter. Instructions don’t correctly indicate that the filter needs soaked in water first before installing. Even with doing this, air pockets stop flow of water through refrigerator. I tried several times to soak it and get all air out. Unfortunately between this and some travel, I missed my return window.

Will never buy this again. Review 18: rating 1.0. it felt like it had little to no resistance when twisting the ... This filter was purchased to replace a UKF8001. Once installed, it leaked as soon as water was dispensed from the fridge. When installing, it felt like it had little to no resistance when twisting the filter on (the old filter has a little resistance to turning, as it should with the seals pressing against it). Review 19: rating 3.0. Didnt last very long :( Unfortunately this only lasted about 3 months before it began dripping during and after using it. The water also started tasting just like what comes out of the faucet with a bitter flavor. Had to break down and pop the high dollars for the whirlpool every drop. This is maybe my 3rd or 4th non whirlpool filter and none of them compare to the flavor or longevity. I was really hoping this time would be different as I hate the high cost of water filters its so ab... Review 20: rating 5.0. It definitely does the job in giving great tasting water while filtering out the unwanted things Have had this filter for one year now. It definitely does the job in giving great tasting water while filtering out the unwanted things. Just now ordering a new filter - noticing within the last few weeks that the flow is getting just a tad slow now. But it has done the job for a year now. Highly recommend.
persona: This customer prioritizes effective water filtration that eliminates unpleasant taste and odor, especially in areas with hard or sulfur-tinged water, and is willing to pay a premium for performance that matches or exceeds expensive name brands, valuing reliability and quality over low price.
reference_output: rating 4.0. No smell No flavor!!! It produces good flavorless water. I on the GA/FL boarder and our water is extremely hard and has a sulfur smell. This removes the smell and flavor. Thought it was a bit more expensive than my previous purchase which is why it’s not 5 star.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. They work Accuracy is a bit off Used these in a airtight dry boxes for my 3-D printer filament as you can see in the pictures my basement humidity levels are at a 54% and in my dry boxes the meter show a dramatic difference in humidity levels due to gel packs inside they do work so I can keep a eye on my rechargeable dry pack to know when it they need to be plugged in and dried out. For the price you really can’t go wrong. They seem to be within 3% of each other when I had them side ... Review 2: rating 1.0. These are horrible they don't really work Neither worked properly. So far off on what it should be Review 3: rating 1.0. Really disappointed Neither one of these units worked. Put both of them in a sealed bag with a new Boveda calibration kit pre-charged at 72% RH. After 24 hours in this setting one of them read 56% RH and the other one at 99% RH. And the temperature was off 10 degrees on one and almost 20 degrees on the other. Put in two Honeywell hygrometers that I use In my humidors and they were exactly 72% humidity. Review 4: rating 3.0. Good humidity sensors... Use a couple of these for a snake habitat and so far they're ok. I've compared the temperature readings to some calibrated thermostats and temperature guns and these have been off by as much as 8 degrees! Granted it's been more accurate but still off by as much as 5 degrees. As humidity sensors, they're great and very spot on. Make sure you place them within good view because you can't really see them from the sides. You have to look at them directly to be ab... Review 5: rating 1.0. Wildly different readings while both in the same humidor. I have both of these sitting in a humidor. While I could certainly expect a few % difference, this isn't even close. Not sure what I'm going to do at this point as far as returning. Review 6: rating 1.0. Do not buy. Highly Inaccurate. Completely wrong readings. Placed both 'cheap' hygrometers as well as a more expensive one in a bag with a boveda 75% humidity control test. My more expensive one was a perfect 75%. The cheap Chinese made hygrometers registered 68% and 72% and there is no way to calibrate. Review 7: rating 1.0. One Worked/ One was Junk I really only needed one and the 1st one I pulled out of the 2pk was junk. I figured it out quickly. When you have 4 thermometers and hydrometer and one of the 2 new meters is 20% off humidity and 15° off temp. That would/could have killed my chicks!
The other one worked great out of the box!
I gave 1 star because of the massive bad read on the 1st one that I was going to use. And if I never checked it before I used it, the consequences could have b... Review 8: rating 3.0. Not very accurate I used to think it was accurate but it's been reading really high humidity and so I brought out the other one, and it's 10% higher, so they don't even match at all. It's 0% humidity outside and I'm running my portable air conditioner, so that made me suspicious about the accuracy. Review 9: rating 1.0. Waste of money. Very inaccurate. Inaccurate at least for temperature. I purchased the two pack for my gecko terrariums. I have one older thermometer but I liked the hydrometer feature on these. Older thermometer and the room thermostat are in sync with each other and generally show the same temp. These are way off. Thermostat and old thermometer show ambient temp in the room to be 66 degrees. This thermometer says the temp IN the HEATED terrarium is 64.9 degrees. I can stick my hand ... Review 10: rating 1.0. Measurements are wrong They are not good folks. Don't buy it. Both ones I received are unreliable. Review 11: rating 2.0. Not accurate These aren’t accurate. The humidity seems fine, compared to two other calibrated humidor hygrometers, but the temperature is 5 degrees lower than the reading on the humidor hygrometers. Review 12: rating 2.0. accuracy doubful placed directly next to each other and the humidity reading is 2% difference Review 13: rating 1.0. Cheap inaccurate product. DO NOT USE IN INCUBATOR OR OTHER HUMIDITY OVER 70%. Item failed (display and accuracy) in the incubator at a humidity of 75%. Review 14: rating 5.0. Accurate, easy to read, bargain! These seem to work very well. We use them in our reptile tank to check the temperature of the basking side and the cooler side. I checked them against the weather channel's outside temperature and it was exactly the same. The double sided foam tape provided holds them onto the aquarium with no issues. The only thing I'd change would be to make the temperature the bigger font and the humidity the smaller number. Review 15: rating 1.0. REVIEW UPDATE 8/2020 **** JUNK DO NOT BUY **** AFTER SEVERAL MONTHS OF OWNERSHIP, THESE AEW JUSR TOTAL JUNK. NOWAY TO ADJUST OR RESET. STAYS AT 40% HUMIDITY EVEN WHEM MY WORKING HYGROMETER IS SET AND WORKING AT 72%. THIS IS MY SECOND SET OF HYGROMETERS, PURCHASED OVER 60 DAYS APART. THEONLY THING PTHATP WORKED IS THAPT IT COST ME A FULL HUMIDOR OF 50 GOOD CIGARS DUE TO IMPROPER HUMIDITY. ****JUNK, DO NOT BUY **** Review 16: rating 2.0. Maybe spend more so you don’t have to buy repeatedly Temperature remains within 2.5 degrees F. However, humidity on both has completely stopped working. One reads 10 and the other 23 (not sure why those numbers) but neither will change. If I take batteries out and back in, they’ll work for about 30sec and go back to those numbers. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect product, not so much perfect delivery Everything seems good. They work well and seem accurate. They look sturdy and exactly how pictured.

Definitely not a product problem, but idk what is up with amazon lately. I live in an apartment complex now. I've ordered 3 things so far. The first package was just set underneath the mailboxes downstairs, nowhere near my door. The second time the package was left on my doorstep, picture was taken, and I recieved a knock at the ... Review 18: rating 2.0. Humidity readings seem to be around +/- 30% or so. Tempature stays accurate. Good for tempature, poor for use in high humidity enviroments Review 19: rating 4.0. It serves it's purpose and for less than $5 Small and mostly accurate. Button cell battery included with each one. I would give 5 stars but it arrived with batteries inside and it has no on/off switch meaning it was operating while in transit. For some reason, that was mildly annoying to me but may be a perk for someone else. Overall, a good product. Review 20: rating 4.0. Great value 11/21/19 review.
Small convenient meter, within .4 of a degree of temperature based on my calibrated temperature gauge and withing. 5 % of humidity.
Has a battery compartment on the back that could use a better clasp but light duty use should be fine. Not recommended for water splash or immersion locations.
Devices come with double sided tape for back, will block battery compartment though.
Clear display although the temp is roughly 50% smaller than th...
persona: This customer prioritizes cost-effective, reliable solutions that deliver proven performance, often choosing third-party or alternative parts based on trusted customer feedback, and values functional accuracy over brand or aesthetic appeal.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Accurate and useful This two-pack is a real bargain. I have a few of the air-tight acrylic cylinder containers for cigars, and these hygrometers ensure the humidity is spot-on. Don't waste your money on more elaborate hygrometers. I also put one of these in my nice wooden humidor that has an analog hygrometer on the front. It's amazing how off that one is.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. These are absolute garbage. I spent the better part of a half ... These are absolute garbage. I spent the better part of a half hour flushing gallons upon gallons through 3 of these complete trash filters. They all sputter and output disgusting looking water. Total waste of money. If you're reading this, do yourself a favor and just don't buy something like this on Amazon - ever. I know it's convenient to have it delivered to your door the same day. However, it's even more inconvenie... Review 2: rating 1.0. Smelly Water VERY DISAPPOINTED!!! I followed the written directions and ran water through the filter for 10 minutes. Now, I have a smell from the filtered water! This NEVER happened before!! Review 3: rating 5.0. Nicely inexpensive filters for our Jenn Aire fridge Nicely inexpensive filters for our Jenn Aire fridge. We have an interior water dispenser and the pressure seems much greater with these filters, which is nice. Review 4: rating 5.0. Better Than OEM Filters I never had a problem with the filters distributed by Whirlpool until they changed "name". I bought 3 of them and all three failed within 2 weeks of purchase. I checked the specs of the Waterdrop filter and they were comparable. I purchased three and installed one of them. It has been going strong for over 3 weeks now with no slow down the in stream like I experienced with the OEM filters. I regularly change my filter every six months and will be purch... Review 5: rating 4.0. So far so good. So far so good. Has increased the water flowing through the dispenser. Have not had it long enough to see how good it really is.
Would like to see how long it lasts before I can write anything more. Review 6: rating 1.0. Not lasting Out of the 3 filters in the pack...2 lasted about a month each and 1 lasted a day or two. After that the water came out very slowly.
I’ve bought these in the past and had much better results. Review 7: rating 5.0. Excellent price. Excellent price....retailers wanted $48 for just ONE filler...got THREE for way less than that! A little research to find the compatible one for my Viking refrigerator, but found it, and it’s working. Review 8: rating 3.0. Ice cubes are almost clear again The filters fit nicely and the water tastes good. The ice cubes are less cloudy, although they still haze up pretty bad well before the intended end of life of the filter. Oh well.

The picture shows a cube from the old filter and immediately after replacement. Much improved, but not bar quality. Review 9: rating 2.0. Meh.. You get what you pay for... Doesn't last as described when you have a 5 person family . Review 10: rating 4.0. so far so good. replaced my filter in my Whirlpool fridge at ... so far so good. replaced my filter in my Whirlpool fridge at a fraction of the cost of the manufacturer version, which was about $50 give or take. No funky taste in the water, no leaks with the filter. Performs just as it should. Time will tell if there are any problems, but like mentioned... so far so good. Review 11: rating 2.0. Made water taste like plastic :( Fit my Viking fridge at a fraction of the cost of OEM filter. LOTS of initial gushing and spluttering when flushing the system after installation (water everywhere!), but then flow was good. Unfortunately this filter made my water taste strongly of plastic, even after a week and a half. I'll be returning these and spending the money on the Viking OEM replacement I should have just gotten in the first place. Review 12: rating 3.0. Leaks water in Jenn Air refrigerators This filter does not work in Jenn Air refrigerators although it is advertised to be a replacement for UKF8001. The water leaks as the filter does not fit snugly. I ordered a 3 pack and tried 2 of them and both leaked water all over my refrigerator. I had to replace it with an Every Drop filter and that worked just fine Review 13: rating 5.0. Great filters for the price The filters fit and are working great will be able to determine later if they last 3 months Review 14: rating 5.0. Good buy, can’t go wrong with price and product Water filter was a perfect fit. It’s easy to install and the taste of the water was noticeably better along with good water pressure.
I have a TDS meter, ( Total Desolved Solids ) which I use periodically to check the water filter performance, I am reading 54 ppm, well under the epa standards.
Will definitely buy again, Great bargain, and Great filter. Review 15: rating 1.0. It was not what I wanted. I thought I ... It was not what I wanted. I thought I was buying a water filter but it was for a drink dispenser. I need to return. Review 16: rating 4.0. does the job I can't comment on scientific quality, but it fits and functions as expected. I bought these solely based on the excellent price. This will save me 70% of historical cost. No more buying from OEM for me. Review 17: rating 5.0. Soak your filter first I recommend you soak it in water before installing. I did it the hard way and it wouldn’t work. Once I soaked the filter it started right away. Then I had to figure out how to remove the “change filter” status. But I googled and held down the light and lock buttons for 3 seconds and it was gone. I love that I can get 3 filters for the price of what the manufacturer would charge you. Review 18: rating 4.0. Looks Just likes the Original Manufacturer Product arrived on time and as described. Haven't used this filter just yet, so I cannot comment on how well it performs. Our first priority is how good the water tastes. That being said, I'll have to leave comments after we use this product. I will state that the build-quality matches that of the original manufacturer. Review 19: rating 4.0. Love those kind of deals Usually buy my filters at Home Depot for over $50
Got these 3 filters for under $40. Love those kind of deals. Review 20: rating 5.0. I tried another brand and I was so disappointed. The water tasted like mildew I tried another brand and I was so disappointed. The water tasted like mildew. It was strange because only a couple of us found it repulsive.... others found it fine. However, these are perfect!!! The water tastes great to everyone! These are just like the original Maytag filters. Thank you!!!!
persona: This customer prioritizes reliable performance and proper fit above all else, and is highly dissatisfied with products that fail quickly or do not function as expected, making them likely to avoid brands with inconsistent quality or compatibility issues.
reference_output: rating 1.0. J U N K I don't remember what brand filter I purchased previously and so I purchased this brand some months back. I tried to use them yesterday and received a real surprise. The first one I installed worked for about 39 seconds and QUIT! I was not able to install the second one--it simply would not fit! In my experience THEY ARE JUNK and I threw them away! What a waste of $$$$.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Magically removes all wrinkles! This item is so wonderful. It's saved me $100's of dollars on dry cleaning. It's so easy to use and works much better than an iron! I've used it on all fabrics including sweaters and wool, and everything looks professionally pressed. I was able to do 7 sets of slacks w/ one tank, so don't let the size fool you. I use to take my wrinkled clothes to the cleaners, but not anymore! Review 2: rating 1.0. messy and painful It belches hot water, the outer housing is hot to the touch, gives about 3 minutes of steam and leaves clothing wrinkled. Total waste of money. Review 3: rating 5.0. GREAT STEAMER! I purchased this little travel steamer to take on my cruise and it worked great. I just used some bottled water in it and it gave me 15 minutes of powerful steam! I was able to steam everything I brought with me without having to stop to refill it. It works GREAT! Thanks for putting such a great product out there. It's really refreshing to purchase a product that actually works!!! Thanks again! Review 4: rating 5.0. love it easy to use not too heavy, i bought 2 already! best hand held steamer ever. Review 5: rating 5.0. best travel steamer This is widely regarded as the best travel steamer, and I would agree. Ordering, shipping, and delivery were great. Review 6: rating 3.0. too expensive, not very efficient I bought this for about $50 last month. It's not very poweful, the area from which the vapor comes out is too small, and it makes it difficult to steam cloths efficiently. the other reason that I gave it a low score, is that if you angle the steamer, there is good chance of water dripping, which can first burn your hands.
I do not recommend this steamer, unless you want it only for on a trip, because it is relatively small and light weight, and ... Review 7: rating 4.0. Nice and portable [[ASIN:B0018ZHHS4 Jiffy Steamer 0601 ESTEAM Travel Steamer, Black]]

Works well. Nice to pop in the suitcase for those needed touch-ups while on a trip. Review 8: rating 2.0. OK for travel or silk. If you've unpacked and found a few wrinkles, this travel steam iron is for you. It also worked pretty well for steaming my silk scarves, which is the reason I purchased it. If you've got lots of time, you can also steam a couple of silk blouses but don't expect dry cleaner results. Again, this is a travel steam iron and not much more. In my experience, it does not work well on anything heavier than silk. Review 9: rating 5.0. Performs as promised! Steamer used on several suits and a wedding dress within a week of receiving. Worked great! Small, easy to use and doesn't take up a ton of storage space. Small enough to easily travel with. Review 10: rating 3.0. Small, but too large to pack The procuct is too large to pack, but too small to be effective at home Review 11: rating 2.0. Not that pleased I always ironed my pants and thought that after steaming them, they would look nicer. Steaming them with this product, did not get rid of the wrinkles in my pants. When I put my pants on after steaming them, the wrinkles were even worse than ironing them. It also took forever to seam a pair of pants. Review 12: rating 5.0. life saver! Recommended: Just took steamer to Las Vegas for a wedding and it did an amazing job on wedding dress and other clothes for the wedding!!!! Everyone that travels needs this product. Review 13: rating 5.0. quick and convenient I gave up ironing over one year ago in favor of a fairly large steam machine. When going on a recent cruise, I purchased this portable steamer hoping that it might work to quickly get out some packing-related wrinkles especially from our formal wear. Well, this steamer worked great! I had no trouble getting it on the ship - although they don't let you bring your own irons on the ships - they have no problem with the steamers. It was easy to use, created a lot... Review 14: rating 4.0. great product! I love this travel steamer. i only use it at home. i don't think it's necessary to get the bigger ones. all it does is take up space. this little steamer work great. It steams clothes nicely, heats up quickly and most importanntly, lasts for years. This is the ONLY portab... Review 18: rating 5.0. Excellent Steamer! I purchased this steamer specifically for my wedding day. My full ball gown, complete with cathedral length train, had been hanging (stuffed) in it's garment bag, hidden from my fiance, for the full month leading up to my wedding day. I knew I would need a good steamer to be used by my dress attendant on my wedding day, so I did extensive research, which led me to the purchase of this Jiffy Steamer. On the big day, I walked into my dressing room to find my gown BEA... Review 19: rating 4.0. Works great for some things I keep the Jiffy steamer in my room so I can dewrinkle clothes last minute and don't feel like running downstairs to the laundry room. Perfect for knits. Works on jeans unless you want a sharp crease. I steam out wrinkles in my cotton scrubs before work or stuff that I didn't get out of the dryer in time. Not good for pressing neck colars, although it works somewhat in front. I use a tongs or clothes pin to pull the sleave or collar taut so I can get t... Review 20: rating 3.0. The Esteam comes with a great instruction and parts manual too. I hate ironing but steam will never replace the iron in my estimation. Wrinkles disappear but clothes don't have that crisp, pressed look you can get with an iron. Having said that, this well-made tool gets rid of ...

reference_output: rating 4.0. Great steamer The steamer worked well to take out the wrinkles in my clothes. Especially useful was the size since I live in an apartment and do not have room for an ironing board.  This does the trick and it is easy to use.

Review 1: rating 1.0. Regret buying this! I have never written a review but I feel compelled to write a review on this product as it was very disappointing!!! I normally put the ice into the water dish for the dogs and my wife and kids use the ice to fill up their water containers before we go play 18 holes. Literally took an hour for about half a glass of ice (regular 16oz glass, nothing huge) and is very damp/watery because the unit fills the reservoir through the spout that pours over the already made ice (design flaw for sure). Machine is also loud and heavy when empty to lift or scoot around. Taller than I thought it would be so is too close to upper cabinets to put on kitchen counter and wit... Review 11: rating 1.0. The hose did not fit tightly enough on the external tank's hose barb, so I ended up putting on a spring action hose clamp. The OD on the tube is 11mm so just pick a clamp that is a little smaller than 11mm and Bob's your uncle! I think the clamp I used had an ID of around 9.5mm when fully closed.

Quality/texture/flavor of the ice is great. It does generate a fair bit of heat so bear that in... Review 13: rating 1.0. Sonic ice! UPDATE:Well this lasted until the 29th of September and stopped working. I’ve contacted Amazo... Review 14: rating 1.0. Put the kettle on the propane stove and in... Review 5: rating 4.0. works as described This works for one cup of coffee. Similar to a drip at half the cost. Review 1: rating 5.0. Superior Product Purchased this range hood for non-ducted installation. I really like the fact you can use halogen bulbs, which make things really bright for cooking. There are 3 fan speeds and 3 light settings. I'm very happy, even though my setup is ventless, it still gets the job done -- quietly, too. The fact you can use this in any configuration is a wonderful feature. Thanks, Broan! Review 3: rating 4.0. BROAN RANGE HOOD Arrived quickly and the hood was securely packed...it is installed in our new house and looks really nice...the highest fan level is noisy but so are all of the ones I researched. Lights are bright and the price on AMAZON was the best out there. Cannot comment yet on the efficiency of the hood because we are not in our house yet...so I am counting on all of the many reviews I have read and BROAN'S reputation. Review 4: rating 5.0. Great HOOD ! I originally purchased this range hood at a local store, after watching the Internet for a few months and not being able to find it at a lower price. Before I even opened it, I decided to check the Internet (one more time) and wouldn't ya know it... there it was on sale on Amazon for $56 bucks cheaper! I ordered from Amazon and then returned the locally bought, unopened one to the brick and mortor store. If this had been just a little difference in price, I would not ... Review 5: rating 5.0. Boran 30" allure II Great looking hood. Not that hard to install. I used two sticks 30 inches long and two pices of 1/4" plywood 6"x12" to support the hood on install. While the wife held the hood in place, I put the plywood on the range and then used the 30" long sticks to hold up the back of the hood. While she held the front of the hood I installed the screws. A little trick that worked great. So far the hood has worked great. I'm more than happy with it. Review 6: rating 5.0. Broan Allure II SS The research was well worth the effort. The Broan Allure II works just fantastic, even without a hood vent. the two charcoal filters, which are not included, and the quiet fan make quick work of the heat and/or smoke. We went with the two halogen bulbs, which are not included, as they gave off much more light in the cooking area. The unit looks great in our newly remodeled kitchen. We coudn't be happier. Review 7: rating 4.0. range hood This hood lives up to expectations for quiet and cleanability. The only problem so far has been that the lights flicker and sometimes go out. They are restored with a little jiggling. This indicates a faulty socket or connection to socket. I'm not about to make a warranty claim, as that would entail removing the hood, shipping it, and eventually re-installing-- not easy or pleasant tasks. Review 8: rating 5.0. Broan Exhaust Fan Great exhaust fan - very quiet. Out of the box it is designed for non vented installation. The fan must be configured (changed) for using vented installation. I recommend getting assistance during the installation when attaching the fan to an overhead cabinet. Connecting the fan to an existing exhaust vent can be difficult. I also recommend purchasing a damper during the installation (to keep cold air out of the house). Review 9: rating 5.0. Really nice! The wall my stove is located on an internal wall that is 25 ft to the nearest external wall, so a real vented system would be too costly ($6-8k) to install. I was very surprised this unit does well with odors and heat. Installation was straight forward - I recommend two people. The vent on high is fairly quiet - not to bothersome to have a conversation while it is on. It is super quiet on low and mid speed. The lights (50w) halogen) is really nice. My only concern ... Review 10: rating 5.0. Great Looks great. Well made. Two filters, on each side of hood, make for excellent removal. Two lights make for excellent visability. Three light and fan selections make for a comforable cooking experience. Worth the money. Review 11: rating 4.0. new stainless range hood My wife and I bought a stainless refrigerator and wanted to replace the hood over the stove. We chose the broan hood. The cfm and the scone was a factor. I installed the hood with a little work whitch wasn't to bad. The hood is working out fine. My wife is happy with the air flow and lighting. It matches well with the rest of our appliances. Review 12: rating 5.0. Broan QS230SS Under Cabinet Range Hood A very nice fairly priced under cabinet rangehood for ducted or non ducted applications. I installed it with the non ducted alternative. The unit installed in a breeze. I had a question on the unit and called Broan's hotline. The customer service was fast, informative and friendly. A wonderful addition to our kitchen. Review 13: rating 3.0. Do not buy for a non-ducted install Just want everyone to know you should read the installation and use instructions on the manufactures website before purchasing a product. That was my mistake. I read lots of reviews to compare the different products I was interested in but not the instructions on the Broan site.

The ventless install seems rather hooky and likely to cause more noise, there should be sones ratings on the non-ducted install. The instructions say if you are ... Review 14: rating 5.0. Great product, gorgeous, very quiet Low speed fan is nearly noiseless; high speed nearly pulls flour off of the counter. Put in some PAR20 LEDs that provide significant task lighting on high and great low-power accent lighting on low. More time-consuming than expected to hang from our apparently non-standard cupboards (could've just drilled through the stainless to make new holes). Watch the stainless steel edges, they are very sharp! Definitely recommend, but take your time and ... Review 15: rating 5.0. Nice Have had for 3 years and works great. "Looks Perfect" according to my wife.
Momma's happy, everyone's happy! Review 17: rating 3.0. could be a little nicer This is not a bad range hood, but the buttons for the fan and light controls make it look very cheep. Review 18: rating 4.0. Broan QS230SS Allure II 30-Inch Under-Cabinet Range Hood, Stainless Steel I purchased this from Amazon and was delivered on time without any problem. It took me about 2 hours to install with my wife's help. I could not remove my range so installing is a little bit awkward as I have to lean to the range trying to push the hood up and it's heavy. I concur with other review that there is damper for the 7" round duct, but only for 3 1/4" x 10" duct. But since I only have ductless option,... Review 19: rating 4.0. Good value for the money Had to deal with a little rattle in the unit, but once remedied, performs well with three speed fan and three light levels. Review 20: rating 4.0. Great Looking and Quiet Very much like this hood. We were in the need for a "recirculating" hood and this did nicely. The fan is reasonably quiet (certainly more quiet than the builder-grade version we did have!) and it looks great! About my only complaint is that "auto" feature - when the hood thinks it's too hot, it'll turn on and exhaust the heat. Not a bad thing, but once it's on, you cannot turn it off until it thinks the temps have dropped enough. I don't l...
persona: This customer prioritizes functional upgrades and ease of installation when replacing kitchen or bathroom ventilation units, values clear product compatibility for ducted setups, and is willing to invest time in installation despite challenges, but expects reliable performance and proper airflow direction without design compromises.
reference_output: rating 3.0. Poor engineering for ducted vent change over. Spent a few hour on the prep and install to replace an old kenmore unit.  Well now I think the Kenmore unit may have performed better, but looked worn out and had no style.  This Broan unit ships prepped for non vented set up.  The change over to vented was easy, and the mounting was typical for a replacement - nothing is as easy as you plan.  Ha!  But the 1st time I tested the vent fan I noticed it is spilling a lot of air out the non vent port on top of the unit.  It should not be doing this at all!!!!  I took it apart and tried to reseal the plastic device that is supposed to block off  the non vent port - I found the plastic blocking device was seated properly.  Put it back together and its only a bit better - still spilling air out the non vent port that should be totally blocked off. The seal of the plastic duct for non-vented application must not be seated properly against the weather stripping on the underside of the hood top.  I guess I will have to disassemble again, and tape off the dang vent openings permanently with aluminum duct tape from the inside of the hood. Poor design - I say this unit is built for non-vented applications first.  I would have paid a bit more $$ for a unit that was dedicated to ducted ventilation only, though it would be cheaper for Broan to produce a unit without the non-venting plastic duct and punched vent holes in the top of the hood.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Good replacement hoses Nothing fancy - just replacement hoses. Much less expensive than those found in B&M stores. They work and so far, have held up well. Review 2: rating 3.0. After some manuvering and new gaskete, was able to get it to stop leaking. Review 3: rating 5.0. These work fine and the price is unbeatable These work fine and the price is unbeatable. Everyone needs to remember though, any of these type hoses are still just rubber hoses subject to deterioration. The braiding would hopefully prevent a huge gusher split from opening up, but all hoses need to be replaced from time to time (I go about three years). Review 4: rating 5.0. Excellent!! This is a super product at a super price!! I think these are the necessary washer hoses to protect your home from unexpected water damage. Review 5: rating 5.0. Nice.. This product seems to be as good, or better than those selling for twice the price elsewhere. I've used these for about 2 months now and have had no issues with fit, leakage, or wear. I would have given the product 5 stars except that these do not include the small in-line screens offered by some which can keep debris out of your washer. I was a bit mystified by the short warranty period. I'm hoping these will last at least as long as my previous non-clad rubber hose... Review 7: rating 3.0. Three Stars Work ok but pretty thin material on the threaded ends Review 8: rating 5.0. Solid build, good price Have been using for several weeks. Initially one of the hoses did leak but I simply tightened the connection. No leak since.

I'm also using plumbers tape on all the connections. No issues since the first one.

I feel safe these will last. Definitely a peace of mind investment over rubber hoses. Review 9: rating 5.0. Nice hoses These last a fairly long time and the nice thing is that if they do spring a leak you don't have a huge spurt of water shooting out all over the place like a straight rubber hose Review 10: rating 5.0. Solved water hammering caused by elbow hoses These hoses solved my water hammering issue. I previously has hoses that had a 90-degree elbow in them, which was causing water hammering with my high-efficiency, front-loading washing machine. Replacing the elbow hoses with straight hoses solved my issue. I also like that these are only 48 in (I actually measure them to be 47 in). Review 11: rating 5.0. Perfect Installed it over a year ago and haven't given it another thought. Perfect Review 12: rating 4.0. Actually a very simple easy task even a man can do (!!) I thought it would be a lot harder than what it ended doing. I, of course, shut off the water off the two faucets, not an easy task and I actually disconnected the hoses from the washer one at a time. They had been there for 18 years and they were very secured at both ends. Once those twisted hosed were free, I reversed the procedure, installing the warm water first since it was below the cold water, tightened it on to the co... Review 13: rating 5.0. washing machine hose 4' kit stainless clad My big box home center stocks 6' hoses and they are twice the price of these 4' hoses.After several family members dealing with burst washer hoses and the water damage, I have been replacing my hoses about every couple of years. This is the first time I've purchased stainless clad hoses. They installed easily and seem to be of good quality. I replace my hoses one-at-a-time to avoid mixing up the hot and cold water supplies. Review 14: rating 1.0. Poor quality I have only had these for a week and already disappointed. They don’t seal properly causing a leak whenever I use my washer. Some water leaked onto the hose in multiple spots and it started to rust. Review 15: rating 3.0. Decent quality, but wish they had 90-degree elbows built-in The quality seems good. However, there are no 90-degree elbows on either end and the hoses are fairly rigid, so they rub up against the drywall. I won't make that mistake again when I replace these in 5 years. Review 16: rating 4.0. Keep the bag in case you need to use the warranty, 5 years is a long time Hoping don't need to use warranty, but I'm learning that sometimes you need it & can't find the info after a couple years. Hoses connected easily & securely to washer & pipes. I had to force them to lay flat before using them though.
persona: This customer values product durability and proper installation, prioritizes long-term reliability over immediate cost, and follows detailed, methodical procedures to ensure a secure setup, indicating a preference for high-quality, long-lasting products that require careful handling and maintenance.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Looks good! Replace your hoses every 5 years, even if you get these. I dated my as you can see to remind me. Will have to see if I have register for the warranty. First make sure the rubber washers are present and push them in flat. I hand tightened them and then marked it with a marker. Then turned it 90 degrees, so full circle is 360, half is 180, and have if that is 90. Slowly turned on the water, ran the water and checked for leaks. I’ll check for a leaks for a bit. I have a few loads to do. Oh ya , wash your hands, this has Prop65 on the back. Look it up if you don’t know what it is.

persona: This customer values functional performance and reliability in kitchen appliances, particularly those that enhance beverage experiences like iced coffee or frozen treats; they prioritize products that deliver consistent results, are easy to maintain, and arrive as described, while remaining open to keeping items even if they don’t fully meet initial expectations.
reference_output: rating 5.0. I'm addicted to Sonic ice and can't get enough If you love Sonic ice as much as I do then you know what it's like when you drop by Sonic for a bag of their ice, rush home toss it in your freezer hoping to keep the refreeze clumping to a minimum......only to have a dickens of a time busting it up....UGH After reviewing several models of counter ice makers I chose this one. It appears the company has been around and the product has several good reviews. It does take quite awhile for the basket to fill so you will need to plan ahead. I usually make several gallon sized bags during the weekend and freeze them for the week. No matter how quickly I transfer the bag to my freezer it will still clump a little. but, nothing like the Sonic ice. A few bangs on your counter will separate each little 'morsel and viola, tiny little chunks of bliss in your glass. Freshly made ice is chewy and exactly like I like it. I recommend this brand and hope you enjoy it as much as I have.

As far as I can tell, all you can do is set the next time it will turn on if it is currently off. I feel spoiled with amazing ice that does not ever disappoint! I also ran the clean cycle on it and made the ice maker cleaner. Do not think twice about an ice maker buy it! Review 11: rating 5.0. Awesome I use Zero water filter first and my ice is Awesome!  I turn it on when I wake up and in 2 hours my 44 oz glass is full.

Do not buy these After only a few months of use I noticed that my top rack was off kilter again. I couldn’t figure out what it was until I saw a big chunk of plastic falling out. This is the inner part of the mechanism. Definitely not quality parts. Review 8: rating 1.0. Parts failed after 4 months use After 4 months of use I noticed the rack was hanging low when pulled out. After looking at the parts that I replaced, both sides were broken. One side had a crack in the center of the main part (circled) and both sides had a ‘button’ that had fractured off that is supposed to stabilize the rear. Very dissatisfied, and wondering if this was a fluke bad set of parts? Certainly this cannot be normal. Review 9: rating 2.0. Buy the 8 or 10 piece instead. You’ll want the new front rack stops not included in this product. Update - plastic wheel clip part broke 8/21. Part lasted 9 months.

First time replacing this part. Wheels are Slightly different from oem, but it works. Dishwasher is only 2 1/2 years old and this was first item to break. Won’t know durability of replacement part until it breaks. Fairly easy to install, approx 30 minutes.

Of note: spend the extra two dollars ... Review 10: rating 5.0. Perfect fit Easy to install received quick Review 11: rating 1.0. Don’t buy. Poor quality. I was a little disappointed that the little plastic clips that attach to the large piece, that actually holds the large piece tight against the rack were not included in this kit. We did make do with the old ones, so I guess if this happens again we will have to look for a kit that replaces everything....not just the wheels and holders for the wheel areas. Review 13: rating 5.0. It fixed my Kenmore dishwasher!! A piece broke on the top rack of my dishwasher three days before extended family members were due to arrive, and the top rack was essentially rendered useless. The local appliance store was going to charge $27 for one tiny piece (about 1" x 2") that may or may not have fixed the problem. A neighbor said I should check Amazon for a whole top rack repair kit, and the rest is history! The kit arrived two days later for only around $22 and free shippin... Review 14: rating 1.0. I was pretty surprised at how well this little fridge worked out. You just can't beat the price. The fridge is quiet and gets cold quickly. We used it for a trade show and had no problems. It's very light weight and durable at the same time. Great for an office, gym, etc. Review 6: rating 4.0. Four Stars Pretty junky, but it works so far. Review 7: rating 4.0. I will update within 30 days regarding temp. Review 8: rating 2.0. Wouldn't recommend, read my review I don't think you could fit even one ice cube tray in the freezer department. Big bulge at bottom area takes up lotsa room. Look at photos of how they stack soda cans in there. One shelf removable. Hard to tell which side has handle. Wouldn't recommend. Review 9: rating 5.0. Five Stars I have only used the unit for two months and it it working as advertised! Review 10: rating 4.0. Four Stars Pretty good but wish the temperature setting were better Review 11: rating 4.0. I don't even know what's going on. Ghosts maybe. This fridge has a mysterious power. Whenever it kicks on the cooling it somehow disables my drawing tablet and pen for about 30 seconds. It's a nice fridge but this unintended side effect is very annoying. Review 12: rating 5.0. Nice fridge but noisy Nice fridge but a little noisy. Its in my office so the noise is no worse than the other machines. But I wouldnt place it in an area where you sleep. Made well, very handsome. Works well Review 13: rating 5.0. Perfect size Excellent fridge for home or office use. It's very useful for drinks and small food items. It's very quiet and it stays really cold. The size is perfect especially if your cubicle is limited in space. Review 14: rating 2.0. It sounds like a plane is taking off The fridge came with scrapes on the top of it. It sounds like a plane is taking off. Other than that it is ok. Review 15: rating 5.0. Five Stars I love the size of it, perfect fit for our upstairs living room. Keeps drinks very cold. Review 16: rating 3.0. But this little fridge does sound like a storm or coffee pot brewing For chill affect this fridge gets 5 stars. It definitely does the job on getting your items cold. However, on noise it gets a 2.5 star. Others reported that the device is load and this is true. For me it is bearable because I'm using it in my office so I'm not trying to sleep or keep the area quite. But this little fridge does sound like a storm or coffee pot brewing. Review 17: rating 5.0. Cold! This fridge was actually bigger than I thought it'd be (I didn't pay attention to the size I guess). But it works great and definitely keeps the items in it cold! Review 18: rating 4.0. So far good A bit Noisy but I guess you get used to it, keeps nice and cold, we'll see how long Review 19: rating 2.0. I bought the Danby one instead and it was much quieter, though the Danby one looks less attractive in terms of appearance.

The packaging also made it very hard to pack it back for return. Review 20: rating 3.0. Not suitable for countertop use The description made me think this was going to be far smaller than it actually is; it's not suitable for countertop use, which is what I wanted it for. It's also quite loud.

I was able to change the settings until I found the right temp for me. It was also well packaged when I got it.

a sheet.
