reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Brown better than White These are not nearly as good as the brown paper Kalita filters that have become very difficult to find for reasons unknown, Review 2: rating 1.0. FAKE Do NOT buy these filters. They are fake! Look at the packaging; it’s slightly different. These filters clog after pouring 200 grams or so, and just don’t work at all. Beware.

Get the real ones from their website. Review 3: rating 5.0. Good paper filters Good quality paper filters for the shorter Kalita pour over coffee makers. These DO NOT fit the OXO pour over coffee makers. Review 4: rating 5.0. Filters are worth the money -- no paper taste The Kalita pour over makes the best coffee, and the filters don't leave a paper taste. Worth the money and the ritual required to make great coffee. Review 5: rating 2.0. Good filter but... The brown basket filters work just as good and are bleach free. And cheaper Review 6: rating 3.0. Insanely priced filters, 3-4 week lead time, so annoying These are great filters if you can afford/obtain them. But they are just paper so why when we have 936 options on Amazon for pop-its or slotted spoons, do we have no decent alternate to these Japan sourced pieces of paper? I have to shop for these 3-4 weeks before I need them to be sure and actually get them before I run out. Review 7: rating 4.0. Looking far and wide for the elusive Kalita I will say other than waiting almost two weeks for these filters, there essential to the Kalita wave. And very nice they don’t leave any aftertaste whether you rinse the filter or not. Just make sure you grind coarse, coarser than a Chemex. It will kinda appear like a brownie when the brew is finished l. I usually sift my fines out, it makes a really fruity cup depending on the beans you have and the freshness. Review 8: rating 1.0. These are different from the Kalita filters I normally receive The dimensions are slightly smaller and the base is narrower by about 5cm.; harder to pour into. Also, the paper is much thicker. I am not sure if these are authentic Kalita? Review 9: rating 5.0. Fits Blue bottle dripper!! Perfect replacement for the expensive blue bottle filters. They fit perfectly and no taste. Review 10: rating 1.0. BEWARE: FAKE/REPLICA BEWARE: This is not the real imported kalita wave filters from Japan. They are thicker and less permeable than the real ones. This slows down the brew time significantly even when you grind the beans very course. Test it your selves and just run regular water, see how slow it flows. It usually has some water slept over even without beans. Review 11: rating 3.0. Expensive but perfect We have (and love) the Kalita 185 drippers and these filters are a perfect fit for those. But they are a lot pricier than generic #2 cone filters which work just fine in the drippers. So mostly we use the generic. Review 12: rating 5.0. Best Filter Form These filters help in one of the best pour-over coffee methods. They don't seem to retain a papery taste, either. Not having to wet them saves you precious minutes en route to your drug of choice--provided, of course, they are matched to a Kalita filter holder and its unique configuration. Review 13: rating 3.0. They're shrinking. I went about a year without buying paper filters when I was using a coffee sock, but the size of these filters has shrunk considerably since the last time I bought them. They now don't even fit the entire basket. Review 14: rating 5.0. Size 185 Fits a Blue Bottle pour over perfectly Got these to try them out as a replacement for the very overpriced blue bottle pour over filters. Couldn’t be happier. They fit perfectly and I cannot tell one bit of difference in the quality of the coffee produced. Review 15: rating 1.0. strong bad flavor!! The filters has a strong bad flavor, I need to wash them twice before I use them for brewing coffee.. Review 16: rating 1.0. Purchased filters in anticipation of coffee dripper which never delivered. bought 10 dollars worth of filers to be used for a 27 dollar coffee dripper. Filers come months ago, dripper never does. What do i need the filters for with out the dripper. So i'm now out nearly 40 dollars for absolutely nothing. thats called laceny where i come from. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect substitute for the Blue Bottle Filters! I was looking for a cheaper alternative to the Blue Bottle Filters, as I already own the Blue Bottle Dripper. These ones are the perfect size and work just as well, for much cheaper. Review 18: rating 2.0. Smelled/Tasted Like Laundry Detergent I was super excited to make coffee and my filters showed up smelling like detergent. I hadn't made coffee with this brewer before so thought it might just be these particular filters. Boy was I wrong. I made a cup and had to pour it out. Even after doing a filter rinse, the detergent taste was definitely there. Hopefully the replacements aren't the same or I will have to order from a specialty shop. Review 19: rating 5.0. OXO Coffee Drip This works perfect for the OXO Pour Over Coffee setup. They no longer make filters (even though they still sell the drip set) so this is a PERFECT match for the glass drip portion. reference_output:
rating 4.0. Works Well but Makes Me Very Nervous to Have Around the House We purchased a whole house humidifier back in January of this year and also purchased this bacteriostatic treatment to go with it. This product comes in a 32 fl oz bottle and is made in the USA. The active ingredients are dimethyl benzyl ammonium chlorides (1.125%) and dimethyl ethylbenzyl ammonium chlorides (1.125%). "Other" ingredients aren't listed but make up 97.75% of the solution.

We've used it several times over the past four and a half months that we have had our humidifier and the treatment seems to work well. It's recommended to use it every time you fill the humidifier but we've used it about once a week and it works just fine but we can always increase the use if we notice any strange smells in the humidifier. There are a lot of warnings on the back of the bottle because this treatment is concentrated and extremely hazardous if it gets on your skin or eyes. For this reason, it makes me very nervous having it around the house since I have kids in the house but we also keep it locked up.

I'd recommend purchasing this to use in a humidifier. It's a good price and works well. It just makes me really nervous to have it in the house but we take every precaution we can by using gloves when handling it and washing our hands thoroughly after using it.

With new filter performance of humidifier greatly improves and reaches set levels very quickly.

I tried another one of these and it was fine. (added April 10, 2018... Review 19: rating 5.0. it starts like a soft blanket and after the winter season it ... persona: This customer values ease of installation, product simplicity, and long-lasting performance, preferring tools-free, reliable solutions that deliver consistent results without maintenance hassles.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Does a great job - well designed I really like the simplicity of this filter. You don't need to turn off anything to install it and you can easily do with with you bare hands without using specialized tools. It has never leaked and does a great job providing water filtered through activated charcoal for about 6 months. I had to admit I was curious about what was inside so I opened an old unit open to check it out. I included an image in the media section. GE uses an interesting approach with this filter. Rather than using a mesh screen with activated charcoal particles in an inner sleeve, they developed a solid, fused charcoal plug that must serve as both the particle and chemical filter. The filter canister is really thick-walled so I imagine it can withstand even intense water pressure. All and all a great product.

reference_output: rating 1.0. 6 months to failure I ordered this to replace the OEM pump that quit working after many years of service.  6 months in, the water stops pumping out.  Upon inspection, the impeller came off the shaft. Its made of very cheap plastic and appears to have flashing left in two of the three windows that look like they should be punched out.  One broke through and may be throwing it off balance.  I was able to snap it back on but nothing holds it in place except for an o- ring and will retry but not holding my breath that it will last more than a few weeks.  This should last way longer that 6 months and could if not made of very thin cheap plastic.
